CPF_DATA.q: How do I get a document that shows I do not have a CPF account?
CPF_DATA.a:
You can login to my cpf digital services via your Singpass. You will see a message "No information can be displayed here as you do not have a CPF account."
 
 You can click on located above the graphic, to save or print a copy.

CPF_DATA.q: What is my CPF account number?
CPF_DATA.a:
If you are currently/were previously a Singapore Citizen or a Permanent Resident, your CPF account number is the Unique Identification Number (UIN) stated on your NRIC, Citizenship Certificate, or entry permit (e.g. S1234567F or T1234567F).
 
 Otherwise, your CPF account number could begin with the letter ‘S’ or ‘T’, followed by the letter ‘A’, ‘B’, ‘C’, ‘D’ or ‘F’ (e.g. SA123456D or TC987654B).

CPF_DATA.q: When do I have a CPF account?
CPF_DATA.a: Your CPF account is automatically created when a first top-up or CPF contribution is received.

CPF_DATA.q: How do I view my CPF Yearly Statement of Account?
CPF_DATA.a:
You can view, print and/or download your CPF Yearly Statement of Account via Singpass login at my cpf digital services. Please note that we do not provide the service of retrieving CPF statements beyond the past 10 years. Please download your statements in a timely manner to ensure that you always have a copy for your own records. To find your downloaded documents on mobile devices, refer to this FAQ.
 
 For non-Singapore Citizens and non-Permanent Residents, please refer to our FAQ for more information.

CPF_DATA.q: I would like a hardcopy of my CPF Yearly Statement of Account (YSOA). Where can I get it?
CPF_DATA.a:
As part of our efforts to go green, CPF Board has transformed the Yearly Statement of Account (YSOA) into a digital statement. We strongly encourage you to view your YSOA online to enjoy a richer and more interactive user experience.
  
 You may log in to my cpf digital services to view, download and save/print your YSOA for the past 10 years by clicking the "PDF icon". To find your downloaded documents on mobile devices, refer to this FAQ.

CPF_DATA.q: How do I read my Digital Yearly Statement of Account?
CPF_DATA.a:
You can view the guide on the Digital Yearly Statement of Account (YSOA) in your preferred language below:

CPF_DATA.q: What is the total employment contribution I received for the year?
CPF_DATA.a:
You can sum up the “Employee Contribution” under the “You” category and “Employer Contribution” under “Employer” category to derive the total employment contributions for the year.
  
 For more details on the rates, see rates for Singapore Citizens and Singapore Permanent Residents.

CPF_DATA.q: How do I derive the breakdown of employer and employee's contributions indicated on my Yearly Statement of Account?
How do I derive the interest indicated in my Yearly Statement of Account?
CPF_DATA.a: Please refer to the CPF interest page for more information on CPF interest. Details on extra interest and how CPF interest rates are determined can be found in our FAQs.

CPF_DATA.q: How can I check my Government cash grants or benefits received for the year?
CPF_DATA.a: We have moved records of CPF-administered cash grants from the Yearly Statement of Account to YourBenefits@LifeSG, as it provides a more comprehensive view of common Government-provided benefits you have received, covering schemes from multiple agencies including CPF Board. You would require Singpass to login. Please refer to the FAQ for more details.

CPF_DATA.q: How do I save or download a copy of my Digital Yearly Statement of Account?
CPF_DATA.a: On the Digital Yearly Statement of Account (YSOA) page, click on “PDF icon” to view, download and save a copy of your YSOA. To find your downloaded documents on mobile devices, refer to this FAQ.

CPF_DATA.q: How do I stop receiving my hardcopy Yearly Statement of Account?
CPF_DATA.a: As part of our efforts to go green, we have transformed the Yearly Statement of Account (YSOA) into a digital statement. If you wish to go green and stop receiving a hardcopy YSOA, please submit a request to us. Singpass login is required.

CPF_DATA.q: I am the appointed donee/deputy for my next-of-kin. How do I request for the Yearly Statement of Account (YSOA) on behalf of my next-of-kin?
CPF_DATA.a:
If you are the appointed donee or deputy, you can consider setting up the Singpass Multi-User SMS 2FA via the links below:
  
 - Application for Donees with LPA
 - Application for Deputies with Court Order
 
 This allows you to conveniently access and view your next-of-kin’s Yearly Statement of Account (YSOA) without having to repeatedly provide supporting documents. 
  
 You will have to provide the necessary supporting documents for the one-time set-up via the links above. Please refer to Singpass FAQs for more information on Multi-User SMS 2FA.
  
 If you are unable to set up the Singpass Multi-User SMS 2FA, you can Write to us to request for your next-of-kin’s statement on their behalf, by providing the following documents:
  
 - For Donee appointed under a Lasting Power of Attorney (LPA): Submit the LPA document and a medical report certifying your next-of-kin’s loss of mental capacity
 - For Deputy appointed by the Singapore Court: Submit the Deputyship court order

CPF_DATA.q: How much extra interest can I earn on my CPF savings?
CPF_DATA.a:
To help boost retirement savings, the Government pays extra interest on the first $60,000 of your combined CPF balances, which is capped at $20,000 for Ordinary Account (OA). Extra interest is structured so that all CPF members benefit, and members with lower balances benefit more. The amount of extra interest paid to you would depend on your combined CPF balances and age.
  
 If you are below 55 years old, you will earn an extra interest of 1% per annum on the first $60,000 of your combined CPF balances (capped at $20,000 for OA). The extra interest earned on your Special Account (SA) and MediSave Account (MA) balances will go to the respective accounts, while the extra interest earned on your OA balances will go into your SA to enhance your retirement savings.
  
 If you are 55 years old and above, you will earn an extra interest of 2% per annum on the first $30,000 and 1% per annum on the next $30,000 of your combined CPF balances (capped at $20,000 for OA). The extra interest earned on your Retirement Account (RA) and MA balances will go to the respective accounts, while the extra interest earned on your OA balances will go into your RA to enhance your retirement savings. If you have joined the CPF LIFE scheme, the extra interest earned will be paid into your RA or accumulated with the interest earned on the CPF LIFE premiums of all CPF LIFE members.
  
 How is my combined CPF balance computed?
  
 Your accounts are used to compute your combined CPF balances in the following order:
  
 - 1st: Retirement Account (RA), including any CPF LIFE premium balance
  
 - 2nd: OA, with a cap of $20,000*
  
 - 3rd: Special Account (SA)
  
 - 4th: MediSave Account (MA)
  
 *A cap of $20,000 from OA is imposed because OA savings are short-term in nature and can be withdrawn on demand for a few purposes such as housing and education.

CPF_DATA.q: How is my CPF interest computed and credited into my accounts?
CPF_DATA.a:
CPF interest is computed monthly. It is credited to your respective accounts by the following year and compounded annually.
  
 CPF balances used for interest computation are affected by the transactions in your account. For instance, contributions (including refunds) received this month start earning interest next month. Withdrawals/deductions in this month will not earn interest from this month onwards.
  
 There are strict procedures in place to ensure the accuracy of members’ CPF accounts and transactions (including interest payments). To further ensure that our system is sound and robust, our statutory auditor performs regular reviews of formulas and computations in our systems for accuracy, and conducts independent verifications of the transactions and account balances.

CPF_DATA.q: Will the interest rates on savings in the Special, MediSave and Retirement Account be maintained at a floor rate?
CPF_DATA.a:
To help CPF members grow their savings, the Government has decided to further extend the 4% floor rate for interest earned on all Special, MediSave and Retirement Account (SMRA) savings for another year until 31 December 2026.
  
 SMRA savings will earn a minimum 4% interest rate per annum until 31 December 2026.
  
 Please refer to our FAQ on how CPF interest rates are determined for more information.

CPF_DATA.q: Who is exempted from receiving CPF contributions?
CPF_DATA.a:
Employees should receive CPF contributions from their employers. However, the following employees are exempted from receiving CPF contributions:
 
 1. Students who fulfil exemption criteria. Find out more about Who you need to pay CPF contributions for (PDF, 0.2MB) under the header “Students”.
 
 2. Foreigners. Persons who are not Singapore Citizens or Permanent Residents.
 
 3. Domestic employees with employment not exceeding 14 hours in any week. Examples include cooks, maids and gardeners.
 
 4. Employees of the United Nations (UN) Organisation, or any agency, institution or related organisation of the UN Organisation that is located in Singapore. Please refer to the UN System Chart (PDF, 0.08 MB) for the list of organisations that are within the United Nations System.
 
 5. Employees of any of the following entities, which is part of the World Bank Group (a specialised agency of the United Nations Organisation), that is located in Singapore:
 i. International Centre for Settlement of Investment Disputes; or
 ii. Multilateral Investment Guarantee Agency
 
 6. Employees of the Organisation for Economic Cooperation and Development, who is assigned to work for the International Energy Agency Regional Cooperation Centre that is located in Singapore.
 
 7. Seamen who are Singapore Citizens and are employed: 
 i. in a Swedish ship on terms and conditions of service applicable to Swedish seamen; or
 ii. in a Norwegian ship on terms and conditions of service applicable to Norwegian seamen; or
 iii. by the East Asiatic Co. Ltd. of Denmark on terms and conditions of the agreement between the Danish Shipowners’ Association and the Seamen’s Union in Denmark.

CPF_DATA.q: I have just obtained my Singapore Permanent Resident status. Do I need to contribute CPF?
CPF_DATA.a:
Yes, CPF contributions are payable from the day you obtained your Singapore Permanent Resident (SPR) status.
 
 In the first two years of obtaining the SPR status, you and your employer will contribute to CPF at graduated rates to help you adjust to the lower take-home pay. However, you and your employer have the option to apply jointly to CPF Board to contribute at higher rates.

CPF_DATA.q: How do I determine the year of my Singapore Permanent Resident status for the purpose of CPF contributions?
CPF_DATA.a:
The year of Singapore Permanent Resident (SPR) status determines your CPF contribution rates as an SPR.
 
 The first year rate applies from the day you obtain your SPR status. This refers to the date indicated on your entry permit (Form 5/5A) issued by the Immigration and Checkpoints Authority of Singapore (ICA). It ends on the last day of the month of your first anniversary of SPR conversion.
 
 The second and third year rates apply from the month following the anniversary of your conversion to an SPR.

 If you have obtained your SPR status on 15 January 2020, the start and end dates for first, second and third year of obtaining SPR status are shown in the table below:

CPF_DATA.q: I have just converted from a Singapore Permanent Resident to a Singapore Citizen. When should I apply the CPF contribution rates applicable to Singapore Citizens?
CPF_DATA.a:
The CPF contribution rates for a Singapore Citizen will apply from the date you have converted to a Singapore Citizen.
 
 For Ordinary Wages (OW), your employer will need to pro-rate your OW and apply the appropriate rates to determine your CPF contributions. Your employer can write to us for assistance on the computation if the OW for the month exceeds the OW ceiling or if the pro-rated wages are below $750.
 
 For Additional Wages (AW), your employer will need to apply the applicable contribution rate depending on when the AW is payable to you.
 
 Example:
 
 Assuming you are in your second year of Singapore Permanent Resident (SPR) status and become a Singapore Citizen on 15 June 2020:
 
 For OW
 
 The second year SPR rate should be applied on the pro-rated wages for the period 1 June to 14 June 2020.
 The Singapore Citizen rate should be applied on the pro-rated wages for the period of 15 June to 30 June 2020.
 For AW
 
 The second year SPR rate should be applied on the AW if it is payable between 1 June and 14 June 2020.
 The Singapore Citizen rate should be applied on the AW if it is payable between 15 June and 30 June 2020.

CPF_DATA.q: What is the minimum age to qualify for CPF contributions?
CPF_DATA.a: As an employee, you are required to pay CPF contributions as long as you are a Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident, and are employed in Singapore under a contract of service.

CPF_DATA.q: I have successfully applied for a new Re-Entry Permit and retained my Singapore Permanent Resident status. How will this impact my CPF contributions?
CPF_DATA.a:
As a Singapore Permanent Resident (SPR), you are required to make CPF contributions on wages earned in Singapore. CPF contributions are not payable for employees who are neither Singapore Citizens nor SPRs.
 
 Determining your Year of SPR status
 Your CPF contribution rates as an SPR depend on how long you have held Permanent Resident status.
 
 First-year rate: Applies from the day you obtain your SPR status, [i.e. the date indicated on your Entry Permit (Form 5/5A) issued by the Immigration & Checkpoints Authority (ICA)] until the last day of the month of your first anniversary of SPR.
 
 Second-year rate: Applies from the month following your first anniversary as an SPR until the last day of the month of your second anniversary.
 
 Third-year rate: Applies from the month following your second anniversary as an SPR onwards.
 
 Example
 If you have obtained your SPR status on 15 January 2026, the start and end dates for first, second and third year of obtaining SPR status are shown in the table below:

CPF_DATA.q: How can I top up to myself, my children or my loved ones' 3 CPF accounts?
CPF_DATA.a:
You can make a top up via Top up your 3 CPF accounts using PayNow QR1.
 
 You will be prompted to key in your Recipient's NRIC/CPF Account Number if you are making a top-up on another person’s behalf.
 
 All applications and payments should reach CPF Board by the last day of the calendar year for it to be counted as the year’s CPF Annual Limit. Backdating of top-ups to previous years is not allowed.
 
 Tax relief is only applicable if you are a self employed-person with assessable net trade income for the year of assessment, when you make a self top-up or receive a top-up from another person.
 
 You can find out more on the different voluntary top-ups.
 
 For self-employed persons, amounts allocated to your MediSave Account from these top-ups will be used to offset your MediSave payable and meet the MediSave requirement for Workfare Income Supplement (if eligible).
 
 1Subsequent or duplicate payments made using the same PayNow QR code will be refunded. To ensure that your cash top-ups are processed successfully, please only scan the PayNow QR code and make payment once. You will need to submit a new application each time you wish to make another top-up.

CPF_DATA.q: How can I top up to myself, my children or my loved ones' MediSave Account?
CPF_DATA.a:
You can make a top-up via Top up MediSave Account using PayNow QR1.
 
 You will be prompted to key in your Recipient’s NRIC/CPF Account Number if you are making a top-up to your loved ones.
 
 You are encouraged to make top-ups early. All applications and payments should reach CPF Board by the last day of the calendar year to enjoy tax relief for the following year’s Tax Assessment. Backdating of top-ups to previous years is not allowed.
 
 Please note that there is no tax relief for top-ups made to your children. Find out more about tax relief for your MediSave top-ups.
 
 You can find out more on which voluntary top-up is applicable to you.
 
 To make MediSave top-ups to yourself or a loved one, you are required to have fully settled or be on a monthly instalment plan for your MediSave payable under the Self-Employed Scheme. You may make payment for your MediSave payable via the payment modes.
 
 1Subsequent or duplicate payments made using the same PayNow QR code will be refunded. To ensure that your cash top-ups are processed successfully, please only scan the PayNow QR code and make payment once. You will need to submit a new application each time you wish to make another top-up.

CPF_DATA.a:
You can make a top up to your MediSave Account using the Top up MediSave Account form and complete your payment using PayNow QR.

CPF_DATA.q: What happens if I do not have the Basic Healthcare Sum when I turn age 65?
CPF_DATA.a: The Basic Healthcare Sum (BHS) is the estimated savings you need in your MediSave Account (MA) to cover your basic subsidised healthcare expenses in old age and is the maximum amount you can hold in your MA. If your balance is less than the BHS at age 65, you are not required to top up your MA. However, if you wish to build up your healthcare savings, you can choose to top up your MA with cash. You can do so via this form to make cash top-ups to your own or your loved ones’ MA.

CPF_DATA.q: If I am 66 years old in 2026 and my applicable Basic Healthcare Sum (BHS) is $75,500, am I allowed to maintain a balance greater than my applicable BHS in my MediSave Account?
CPF_DATA.a: You can only save up to your applicable Basic Healthcare Sum (BHS) in your MediSave Account (MA), which is the maximum amount you can hold in your MA. The BHS is designed to cover your basic subsidised healthcare needs in old age and to prevent the excessive accumulation of funds in your MA.

CPF_DATA.q: What is the Basic Healthcare Sum?
CPF_DATA.a:
The Basic Healthcare Sum (BHS) is the estimated savings you need in your MediSave Account (MA) to cover your basic subsidised healthcare needs in old age and is the maximum amount you can hold in your MA.
 
 For CPF members below age 65, the BHS is adjusted annually to keep pace with rising healthcare consumption. This ensures that the BHS remains relevant when each cohort reaches retirement age.
 
 Once CPF members reach age 65, their BHS is fixed and will remain unchanged for the rest of their lives.
 
 For CPF members below age 65 in 2026
 The prevailing BHS is $79,000 , and will be adjusted yearly.
 
 For CPF members who turn age 65 in 2026
 The cohort BHS is $79,000 and will remain fixed thereafter.

CPF_DATA.q: Is there a maximum amount that I can save in my MediSave Account?
CPF_DATA.a:
You can save up to the prevailing Basic Healthcare Sum (BHS) in your MediSave Account (MA) if you have not turned age 65. It is the maximum amount you can hold in your MA.
 
 From 1 January 2026, the BHS is $79,000 for all CPF members aged 65 years and below. The BHS applicable to you will be adjusted yearly to keep pace with rising healthcare consumption until you turn age 65. For example, if you are aged 65 years old in 2026, your applicable BHS will be fixed at $79,000 for the rest of your life. CPF_DATA.q: If I work after age 65, do I still need to contribute to my MediSave Account?
CPF_DATA.a: As long as you continue to work, you will need to contribute to your MediSave Account (MA). These contributions will accumulate up to the Basic Healthcare Sum and help cover your medical expenses during retirement.

CPF_DATA.q: How is the Basic Healthcare Sum determined?
CPF_DATA.a: The Basic Healthcare Sum is an estimate of your likely medical expenses from age 65 onwards. It is determined based on past trends in MediSave usage among the elderly and is intended to cover your own basic subsidised healthcare expenses, as well as health and long-term care insurance premiums during old age.

CPF_DATA.q: Why is the Basic Healthcare Sum adjusted yearly?
CPF_DATA.a:
The Basic Healthcare Sum (BHS) is adjusted annually for cohorts below age 65 to ensure it remains relevant and keeps pace with rising healthcare consumption, especially with increasing healthcare costs and expansions in MediSave usage.
 
 For each cohort, the BHS is fixed from age 65 onwards and remains the same for the rest of their lives. This helps members save adequately for their basic subsidised healthcare needs in old age, while providing certainty on the amount they need to set aside in their MediSave Account.

CPF_DATA.q: What is the Basic Healthcare Sum when I turn 65 years old?
CPF_DATA.a:
For each cohort turning 65 years old, the applicable Basic Healthcare Sum (BHS) is fixed at that year's amount for the rest of their life. It is the maximum amount you can hold in your MediSave Account (MA). For example, if you are 65 years old in 2026, your applicable BHS is $79,000 and will remain unchanged for life.

CPF_DATA.q: Do I have to top up my MediSave Account to the Basic Healthcare Sum if I start withdrawing my CPF savings from age 55?
CPF_DATA.a: You do not need to top up your MediSave Account (MA) when you begin withdrawing your CPF savings from age 55. The Basic Healthcare Sum (BHS) is the estimated savings you need in your MA to cover your basic subsidised healthcare needs in old age and is the maximum amount you can hold in your MA.

CPF_DATA.q: If I do not have my cohort’s Basic Healthcare Sum when I reach my Payout Eligibility Age and I intend to start receiving my retirement payouts, do I need to top up my MediSave Account?
CPF_DATA.a: You do not need to top up your MediSave Account (MA) to the Basic Healthcare Sum (BHS) in order to receive your retirement payouts. The BHS is the estimated savings you need in your MA to cover your basic subsidised healthcare needs in old age and is the maximum amount you can hold in your MA.

CPF_DATA.q: As an employee, how much do I contribute to my MediSave Account (MA)?
CPF_DATA.a: A portion of your monthly CPF contributions is allocated to your MediSave Account depending on your age group. You can refer to the CPF contribution and allocation rates.

CPF_DATA.q: I am a government pensioner receiving lifelong medical benefits. Is the Basic Healthcare Sum (BHS) applicable to me?
CPF_DATA.a:
The Basic Healthcare Sum (BHS) for government pensioners differs from that of other CPF members in the same cohort, depending on the coverage of their Post-Retirement Medical Benefits Schemes.
 
 The BHS does not apply to government pensioners under the Fixed Amount on Ward Charges Scheme (FAW). All working contributions allocated to their MediSave Account (MA) and any MediSave top-ups will be transferred to their other CPF accounts.
 
 Pensioners under the Co-payment on Ward Charges Scheme (CPW) have a lower BHS than other CPF members in their cohort. Once this BHS is reached, any working contributions allocated to their MA and any MediSave top-ups will be transferred to their other CPF accounts.
 
 Pensioners under the Comprehensive Co-Payment Scheme (CCS) and MediSave-cum-Subsidised Outpatient (MSO) Scheme will have the same BHS as other CPF members in their cohort.
 
 Dependants of pensioners will also have the same BHS as other CPF members in their cohort. They can refer to the FAQ ‘What is the Basic Healthcare Sum when I turn 65 years old?’ to find out their applicable BHS.

CPF_DATA.q: What is the minimum balance I need to set aside in my MediSave Account?
CPF_DATA.a: There is no minimum amount required to set aside in your MediSave Account (MA). A portion of your working contributions will be allocated to your MA until your balance reaches the Basic Healthcare Sum (BHS). The BHS is the estimated savings you need in your MA to cover your basic subsidised healthcare needs in old age and is the maximum amount you can hold in your MA. Any amount above the BHS will be channelled to your other CPF accounts, depending on your age. You can find out how MediSave contributions above the BHS are channelled to other CPF accounts via this link

CPF_DATA.q: I have saved the Basic Healthcare Sum (BHS) in my MediSave Account (MA). What happens to the savings in my MA above the BHS?
CPF_DATA.a:
Any savings in your MediSave Account (MA) above the Basic Healthcare Sum (BHS) will be channelled to your CPF Special Account (SA) or Retirement Account (RA), depending on your age. The BHS cap and overflow arrangement are designed to prevent excessive saving in your MA while helping to supplement your retirement savings, which can be withdrawn according to the CPF withdrawal rules.
 
 If your MA savings exceed the BHS, the excess will be channelled to your Ordinary Account (OA) if you are:
 Below 55 years old and have met the current Full Retirement Sum (FRS) (consisting of your SA balance and any active CPFIS-SA investments) in your SA; or
 55 years old or older and have set aside your FRS (which can be entirely in cash or a combination of property and cash) in your RA.
 Savings in the OA can be used for other purposes, such as housing repayment or withdrawn if you meet the withdrawal requirements.

CPF_DATA.q: What happens if I do not have the Basic Healthcare Sum when I turn age 65?
CPF_DATA.a: The Basic Healthcare Sum (BHS) is the estimated savings you need in your MediSave Account (MA) to cover your basic subsidised healthcare expenses in old age and is the maximum amount you can hold in your MA. If your balance is less than the BHS at age 65, you are not required to top up your MA. However, if you wish to build up your healthcare savings, you can choose to top up your MA with cash. You can do so via this form to make cash top-ups to your own or your loved ones’ MA.

CPF_DATA.q: If I am 66 years old in 2026 and my applicable Basic Healthcare Sum (BHS) is $75,500, am I allowed to maintain a balance greater than my applicable BHS in my MediSave Account?
CPF_DATA.a: You can only save up to your applicable Basic Healthcare Sum (BHS) in your MediSave Account (MA), which is the maximum amount you can hold in your MA. The BHS is designed to cover your basic subsidised healthcare needs in old age and to prevent the excessive accumulation of funds in your MA.

CPF_DATA.q: How do I inform CPF Board of a change in my address?
CPF_DATA.a:
By default, CPF letters are sent to your residential address on your Singapore Identity Card. If you are residing at a new address and have been receiving your CPF letters at your old residential address, please apply for the change of your residential address (local or overseas) via Immigration & Checkpoints Authority’s (ICA) e-Services using your Singpass.
 
 Participating government agencies and statutory boards (including CPF Board) will be informed of the change in address through the One-Stop Change of Address Reporting Service (OSCARS) within one day. You will not be receiving an additional acknowledgement from CPF Board for updates via OSCARS.
 
 Please visit ICA website for more information.

CPF_DATA.q: When and how should I inform CPF Board about changes to my particulars?
CPF_DATA.a: For Singapore Citizens/Permanent Residents, you do not need to inform us separately as your latest particulars will automatically be updated in our records.

CPF_DATA.q: How can I inform CPF Board if I changed my name?
CPF_DATA.a:
We use the principal name registered in your NRIC to transact with you. Principal name usually excludes variants such as hanyu pinyin, married name and other aliases.
 
 If you have updated your principal name registered in your NRIC recently, you do not need to inform us separately. Your name will automatically be updated in our records.
 
 If you have not updated the name registered in your NRIC or would like to transact with us using your hanyu pinyin, married or other aliases, you will need to update your principal name in your NRIC first.

CPF_DATA.q: What should I do if I receive mails from CPF Board that do not belong to me and my family member(s) at our address?
CPF_DATA.a: If you received mails from CPF Board that do not belong to you and your family member(s) at your address, please state the reason for return, e.g. 'no such person' or 'change of owner', and drop the mail into SingPost's "Return Mail Here" mailbox. The undelivered mail will be returned to us.

CPF_DATA.q: How long does it take to update my particulars?
CPF_DATA.a:
Please refer to the table for the service standards in updating your particulars.
 
 Service Normal Processing Time
 Update of Personal Particulars for Singapore Citizens/Permanent Residents 3 working days
 Merging of CPF accounts 7 working days

CPF_DATA.q: Do I need to inform CPF Board of my new Singapore Citizenship or Permanent Residency?
CPF_DATA.a:
You do not need to inform us of your latest particulars when you become a new Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident. Your Singapore identity card number will be used as your CPF account number to transact with us.
 
 However, if you are already holding an existing CPF account (example: SA123456D or TC987654B), you would need to inform us so that we can combine your CPF accounts. Please submit the following documents/information to us where applicable by post or in-person at our CPF Service Centres.
 
 To facilitate your transaction with us, we will combine your CPF accounts within seven working days after we have successfully verified your identity.
 
 Recently obtained Singapore Citizenship
 
 If you are:
 Documents/Information Required
 Additional document(s)
 Ex-Malaysian
 1. Both sides of your Singapore Identity Card
 2. Both sides of your Singapore Citizenship Certificate
 3. Change of name document (example: deed poll), if you have changed your name
 4. Your email address and contact number (we will contact you if we require more information)
 NA
 Ex-foreigner (excluding ex-Malaysian)
 Your old passport page(s) showing your personal particulars and passport number registered with us earlier
 Recently obtained Permanent Residency
 Malaysian
 1. Both sides of your Singapore Identity Card
 2. Your current passport page(s) showing your passport number, personal particulars and re-entry permit or Form 7 issued by Immigration and Checkpoint Authority
 3. Change of name document (example: deed poll), if you have changed your name
 4. Your email address and contact number (we will contact you if we require more information)
 Both sides of your Malaysia Identity Card
 Foreigner (excluding Malaysian)
 Your old passport page(s) showing your personal particulars and passport number registered with us earlier
 
 By Post
 Our mailing details are as follows:
 Central Provident Fund Board
 Accounts Information & Services Department
 Privy Box No. 920651
 Singapore 929292
 
 Please note that your documents must be certified true by an official from a Singapore Overseas Mission with his/her official seal/stamp duly affixed. You may wish to visit the Singapore Ministry of Foreign Affairs website to locate the Singapore Overseas Mission nearest to you.
 
 In-person at our CPF Service Centres
 Please bring along your original documents and book an appointment to visit us at our CPF Service Centres.

CPF_DATA.q: How do I inform CPF Board if I have changed my contact details?
CPF_DATA.a:
Notifications will be sent to you whenever you make a transaction. Also, we may contact you to perform further verification checks on your transactions for added security. Hence, it is important to check and keep your contact details updated.
 
 You can update your mobile number and/or email address via the Account Settings page with your Singpass. An SMS or email One-Time Password (OTP) will be sent to your new mobile or email address for verification before the changes can be submitted.
 
 Please note that any changes to your contact details will be subject to a 12-hour cooling period and you may be subject to enhanced authentication measures to verify your identity.
 
 You can also watch the instructional video below on how to update your contact details with CPF Board.

CPF_DATA.q: Can I make further changes to my contact details while my current request is still in the 12-hour cooling period?
CPF_DATA.a:
Yes, you can submit further changes to your contact details via the Account settings page with your Singpass.
 
 Please note that your most recent request will supersede the previous submission and the changes will take effect after the 12-hour cooling period.

CPF_DATA.q: What should I do if I receive a notification that is not meant for me?
CPF_DATA.a:
This could happen because:
 
 You are sharing contact detail(s) with your next-of-kin for handling of CPF matters; or
 Your contact detail is a recycled mobile number/email address registered by another member with us previously; or
 You are using a common business mobile number/email address shared within a company.
 If you did not intentionally share your contact details with another member or wish to stop this arrangement, please write to us with a screenshot of the notification you have received.

CPF_DATA.q: Would other members receive my CPF notifications if they have registered the same contact detail(s) as me?
CPF_DATA.a:
Members will receive CPF notifications at their email address and/or mobile number registered with us.
 
 Other members can receive your CPF notifications only if they share the same email address or mobile number as you.

CPF_DATA.q: I have reviewed my contact detail(s) and am aware that it is also registered under another family member. Can we continue to share contact detail(s)?
CPF_DATA.a:
We understand that some CPF members require help from family members on their CPF matters, and could have registered the same contact details with us. 
 
 No further action is needed if your contact details are still valid.
 
 Otherwise, you can update your new contact details via the Account settings page with your Singpass.

CPF_DATA.q: Can I view my child's CPF statements online?
CPF_DATA.a:
You can view your child’s Transaction History statement as well as the Healthcare Payments and Claims statement (if applicable) for the last 15 months online if:
  
 1. You and your child are Singapore Citizens or Permanent Residents, and
  
 2. Your child is below the age of 21 and has received CPF contributions (including Government Grants such as MediSave Grant for Newborns).
  
 Simply login to my cpf Home > Child Dashboard with your Singpass to access this information.
  
 If you do not see your child’s name listed in the Child Dashboard, you can write to us with the following supporting documents and we will be happy to assist.
  
 Supporting documents required:
 Birth certificate of your child
 Citizenship certificate if your child was a foreigner at birth (where applicable).

CPF_DATA.q: If I am granted sole custody of my child following a divorce, can I request to remove my ex-spouse's access to our child's CPF statement?
CPF_DATA.a: Yes, you can write to us using your Singpass and attach the Court Order as a supporting document.

CPF_DATA.q: When will my CPF account be closed if I am not a Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident?
CPF_DATA.a:
If you are not a Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident before 1 April 2024, your CPF account will be automatically closed in April 2024 and any remaining savings will stop earning the prevailing CPF interest.
 
 As a concession, these savings will earn interest similar to commercial bank interest rates until 31 March 2027. Subsequently, no interest will be paid. You are encouraged to transfer the remaining savings to your bank account as soon as possible.
 
 If you renounced Singapore Citizenship or Permanent Residency from 1 April 2024, please close your CPF account and transfer your CPF savings to your bank account when your renunciation is completed, i.e. when you are no longer a Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident.
 
 If you do not do so, your CPF account will be automatically closed in the month following your renunciation, i.e. if you renounced your Singapore Citizenship or Permanent Residency in April 2024, your account will be closed in May 2024. Any remaining savings will stop earning the prevailing CPF interest.
 
 As a concession, these savings will earn interest similar to commercial bank interest rates until 31 March 2027. Subsequently, no interest will be paid.

CPF_DATA.q: Can I still request information regarding my CPF balances, CPF interest earned or, my CPF transaction statement after renunciation of my Singapore Citizenship/ Permanent Residency?
CPF_DATA.a:
If you have just renounced your Singapore Citizenship/ Permanent Residency, please download any CPF information required, and settle any housekeeping matters, while you still have access to your Singpass account, via my cpf digital services or CPF Mobile app. 
 
 Due to difficulties in authenticating a caller’s identity over the phone and email, we seek your understanding that the Board is unable to disclose any information relating to a specific CPF account over phone or email. This is for your own protection.
 
 The Board would like to assure that your full CPF balances will be transferred to your bank account upon your request, after the closure of your CPF account.

CPF_DATA.q: What happens when my CPF account is closed after my renunciation of Singapore Citizenship/ Permanent Residency and I have yet to transfer my CPF savings to my bank account?
CPF_DATA.a:
Once your CPF account is closed, any remaining savings in your account will stop earning the prevailing CPF interest.
 
 As a transitory measure, these savings will earn interest based on the 3-month average of the three local banks’ savings account interest rates. This is because non-Singapore Citizens and non-Permanent Residents can transfer these monies to their bank account at any time. The applicable rate is 0.05% per annum from April 2024 and will be fixed for a 3-year period until 31 March 2027. From 1 April 2027, any remaining savings will no longer earn any interest.
 
 You can still transfer the remaining savings to your bank account at any time.
 
 Your participation in all the schemes administered by the CPF Board will also cease. Please refer to the section "Schemes' participation upon account closure" for more information regarding schemes’ participation when your CPF account is closed.

CPF_DATA.q: What happens to my CPF account if I am no longer a Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident?
CPF_DATA.a: Your CPF account will be closed, and you are required to transfer your CPF savings to your bank account. To transfer your CPF savings, please refer to the steps mentioned in this FAQ.

CPF_DATA.q: How can I inform CPF Board of a change in my mailing address if I am not a Singapore Citizen (SC) /Permanent Resident (PR)?
CPF_DATA.a: With the closure of your CPF account as a non-Singapore Citizen (SC)/non-Permanent Resident (PR) from 1 April 2024, you do not have to inform CPF Board of any changes in your mailing address. You may refer to our webpage for more information on account closure.

CPF_DATA.q: Are all non-Singapore Citizens and non-Permanent Residents eligible for account closure?
CPF_DATA.a: Yes, all non-Singapore Citizens and non-Permanent Residents should close their CPF account and transfer their CPF savings to their bank account by submitting the account closure form.

CPF_DATA.q: I am not a Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident. I have recently checked my CPF account and noticed that my CPF balance is $0. Why is this so, as I have yet to withdraw my CPF monies?
CPF_DATA.a:
As you are no longer a Singapore Citizen / Permanent Resident, your CPF account has been automatically closed in the month following your renunciation. Any remaining CPF savings have been transferred out of your CPF account into the General Moneys of the Fund (GMF)* and stopped earning the prevailing CPF interest.
 
 As a concession, the savings will earn interest similar to commercial bank interest rates until 31 March 2027. You are encouraged to transfer your savings to your bank account as soon as possible.
 
 To transfer your CPF savings to your bank account, please refer to the steps, under “Take Action”.
 
 * The GMF is an account where CPF Board holds unclaimed monies on behalf of CPF members, nominees or beneficiaries.

CPF_DATA.q: What happens to my Dependants’ Protection Scheme (DPS) cover when my account is closed after my renunciation of Singapore Citizenship/ Permanent Residency?
CPF_DATA.a:
Your Dependants' Protection Scheme (DPS) coverage will be terminated on the date of Singapore Citizenship/Permanent Residency renunciation, as the scheme is only applicable to Singapore Citizens and Permanent Residents.
 
 Your DPS insurer will refund any unused DPS premiums for the current policy year to your CPF account. The refund amount is calculated on a pro-rated basis. This means that you will receive a refund for the remaining months of coverage in your current policy year after your DPS termination date. For example, if your policy year runs from January to December and your DPS is terminated in June, you would receive a refund for the unused premium covering July to December.
 
 The unused DPS premiums will be paid to you together with your CPF savings when you transfer your CPF savings to your bank account.
 
 If you wish to find out more about your specific refund amount or have questions about the calculation, please contact your DPS insurer, Great Eastern Life at greateasternlife.com > Contact us.

CPF_DATA.q: What happens to my Home Protection Scheme (HPS) cover when my account is closed after my renunciation of Singapore Citizenship/ Permanent Residency?
CPF_DATA.a: When you have renounced your Singapore Citizenship or Permanent Residency, your Home Protection Scheme (HPS) cover will cease upon the sale of your HDB flat or at the end of the current HPS policy year, whichever is earlier. There will be no further renewal of your HPS cover, even if you are in the process of selling your HDB flat, as the scheme is only applicable to Singapore Citizens and Permanent Residents.

CPF_DATA.q: What happens to my MediShield Life/ElderShield plans when my account is closed after my renunciation of Singapore Citizenship/ Permanent Residency?
CPF_DATA.a:
If you are a non-Singapore Citizen/non-Permanent Resident covered under MediShield Life/ElderShield before 1 April 2024,
 
 (i) your cover(s) will cease unless you inform us of your intention to continue your cover(s) via Write to Us and make alternative payment arrangements for the premiums to be paid.
 
 (ii) ElderShield policyholders with paid-up policies will remain covered. No further action is required.
 
 For more details on MediShield Life
 
 For more details on ElderShield

CPF_DATA.q: What happens if I used my Retirement Account (RA) savings to purchase annuity(ies) under the Retirement Sum Scheme (RSS) or used my annuity(ies) to apply for exemption from setting aside a retirement sum?
CPF_DATA.a: Once your CPF account is closed and your CPF savings are transferred to your bank account, the surrender value of your annuity, if any, will be paid directly to you by your insurer, in the event of termination of the annuity(ies), or to your beneficiary upon your demise.

CPF_DATA.q: What happens to my CPF savings if I have an outstanding CPF education loan when my account is closed after my renunciation of Singapore Citizenship/ Permanent Residency?
CPF_DATA.a:
If you have used another CPF member’s CPF savings for your education and have an outstanding CPF education loan owed to him, your CPF savings may be used to pay the education loan before the remaining CPF savings are transferred to your bank account. In the event that your CPF savings are not sufficient to cover your full education loan, your repayment liability remains even after your CPF account is closed.
 
 If you have used your CPF savings for your education or your child/spouse/siblings/relative’s education, the repayment of all such outstanding CPF Education Loans to you will be waived.

CPF_DATA.q: What happens to the top-ups I had received in my Special or Retirement Account when my account is closed after my renunciation of Singapore Citizenship/ Permanent Residency?
CPF_DATA.a: Any remaining monies which you have received through CPF transfers and cash top-ups will be paid to you, along with your CPF savings, when you transfer your savings to your bank account. This is subject to safeguards to prevent premature withdrawals.

CPF_DATA.q: What happens to my CPF LIFE plan when my CPF account is closed after my renunciation of Singapore Citizenship/ Permanent Residency?
CPF_DATA.a: Your CPF LIFE plan will be terminated, and you will receive your CPF LIFE premium balance (if any) together with your CPF savings when you transfer your savings to your bank account.

CPF_DATA.q: Do I have to refund the sale proceeds when I sell my property after my CPF account is closed upon my renunciation of Singapore Citizenship/ Permanent Residency?
CPF_DATA.a: Yes, you must refund your CPF principal amount withdrawn with accrued interest or amount you have pledged to refund if you have not transferred your CPF savings to your bank account upon renunciation, by the time you sell your property.

CPF_DATA.q: What happens to my investments when my CPF account is closed after my renunciation of Singapore Citizenship/ Permanent Residency?
CPF_DATA.a:
The treatment of your investments when your CPF account is closed will depend on the investment scheme you have participated in.
 
 CPF Investment Scheme – Ordinary Account (CPFIS-OA)
 We will inform the agent bank to close your CPF Investment Account. The agent bank will then contact you for the withdrawal# of your investments and cash balance (if any). Your investments will be transferred to your own name and you may thereafter continue servicing your investments using cash or liquidate them as you wish and have the sale proceeds paid to you directly.
 
 Once transferred, your CPFIS investments and cash balance in your CPF Investment Account will no longer be protected from any claims by your creditors and/or the Official Assignee.
 Please notify the Board via Write to Us if you have not received your cheque/ Cashier Order within two months from the point of form submission.
 # Central Depository (Pte) Ltd (“CDP”) imposes a transfer fee of $10.00 (excluding GST) for every share counter transferred from your CPF Investment Account to your CDP Account.
 
 CPF Investment Scheme – Special Account (CPFIS-SA)
 We will inform your product provider(s) to transfer your investment to your own name. Thereafter, you may liquidate them as you wish and have the sale proceeds paid to you directly.
 
 Once transferred, your CPFIS investments will no longer be protected from any claims by your creditors and/or the Official Assignee.
 
 Special Discounted Shares (SDS) Scheme
 Your discounted Singtel shares will be automatically liquidated in the month following the automatic closure of your CPF account. The sales proceeds will be held together with your CPF savings, which you can transfer to your bank account at any point in time.
 
 If you do not wish for your discounted Singtel shares to be automatically liquidated, please liquidate them before you renounce your Singapore Citizenship or Permanent Residency.

CPF_DATA.q: What happens to my CPF savings if I have unpaid taxes, MediShield Life premiums, CareShield Life premiums or outstanding education loan owed to a CPF member when my account is closed after my renunciation of Singapore citizenship/ permanent residency?
CPF_DATA.a:
If you have unpaid taxes/MediShield Life premiums/CareShield Life premiums/outstanding education loan owed to a CPF member, we may recover the unpaid amount from your CPF savings when your CPF account is closed.
 
 You are encouraged to settle any outstanding liabilities (e.g. insurance, tax and housing liabilities) before your CPF account is closed.

CPF_DATA.q: What if a non-Singapore Citizen or non-Permanent Resident with a CPF account has passed away before he had closed his account and transferred all his CPF savings?
CPF_DATA.a: The next of kin will have to inform CPF Board of his passing with the original death certificate. Thereafter, the deceased’s remaining savings will be distributed to his beneficiaries, depending on whether he had made a valid CPF nomination before his passing.

CPF_DATA.q: What can I do if I have not received my cheque/Cashier’s Order or my cheque/Cashier’s Order has expired?
CPF_DATA.a: Please notify the Board via Write to Us if you have not received your cheque/ Cashier Order within two months from the point of form submission.

CPF_DATA.q: Can I choose to receive my CPF savings partially when my CPF account is closed after my renunciation of Singapore citizenship/ permanent residency?
CPF_DATA.a: No, your CPF savings will be paid to you in full.

CPF_DATA.q: When can I expect to receive my CPF savings upon account closure?
CPF_DATA.a:
The time taken to receive your CPF savings depends on the type of application submitted.
 
 Type of application Normal Processing Time 
 Online applications (for Malaysians only) Six weeks from the point of submission but may take longer if additional supporting documents are required. 
 Other application mode:
 
 by post
 at the CPF Service Centre
 via Write to Us 
 12 weeks from receipt of all documents. 
 
 Please note that this excludes the time taken to transfer your CPF savings to your bank account, which depends on the payment mode: 
 
 Bank transfer via Interbank GIRO to a bank account in Singapore: Two working days;
 Telegraphic transfer: Five to 12 working days
 To keep your savings safe, CPF Board carefully checks every application, especially for those without Singpass to ensure that the CPF savings are disbursed correctly.
 
 We are working hard to process your application, so please wait at least three months from your application date before checking with us on your application status. This helps us focus our efforts on processing the applications received. We seek your understanding that we may not be responding to any enquiries prior to that.

CPF_DATA.q: I am not a Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident, how do I receive my CPF savings?
CPF_DATA.a:
You will need to submit an application to receive your CPF savings if you are no longer a Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident. For details on the various application modes, please refer to this FAQ. When submitting your application, you may choose to receive your CPF monies via one of the following two options:
 
 1. Bank transfers via Interbank GIRO (IBG) to your Singapore bank account
 
 2. Telegraphic transfer to your bank account overseas
 
 Please ensure that the bank accepts payment in Singapore dollars. Otherwise, we will convert the payment to US dollars. You will need to bear all bank charges, including any foreign exchange rate differences and conversion charges:
 
 i. for telegraphic transfer to an overseas bank account
 ii. if you provide insufficient bank account details resulting in an unsuccessful telegraphic transfer.

CPF_DATA.q: How do I cancel my Permanent Resident status if I intend to close my CPF account upon leaving Singapore?
CPF_DATA.a: As cancellation of Permanent Resident status is administered by the Immigration & Checkpoints Authority (ICA), you may submit your request via ICA | Feedback

CPF_DATA.q: How can I get the letter from Immigration & Checkpoints Authority confirming the cancellation of my Singapore Permanent Residency?
CPF_DATA.a:
To obtain the letter from Immigrations & Checkpoints Authority (ICA) confirming the cancellation of your Singapore Permanent Residency, you may submit your request via ICA | Feedback.
 
 *If you are a Malaysian, you are not required to obtain the letter from ICA.

CPF_DATA.q: I am not a Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident. How do I close my CPF account and transfer my CPF savings to my bank account?
CPF_DATA.a:
If you are not a Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident and would like to close your CPF account, you can do so through one of the following methods:
 
 For Malaysians For all other non-Singapore Citizens and non-Permanent Residents
 Online Form 
 Complete the online form.
 
 You will need your CPF Account Number to submit your application via the online form. If you do not have your CPF Account Number*, please apply via the alternate ways listed below.
 
 The Board may request additional supporting documents to assess your eligibility.
 
 *Please note that we are unable to disclose your CPF Account Number over the phone and email.
 
 Not Applicable
 With Singpass 
 Submit your application via Write to Us, which requires you to login with your Singpass.
 
 For more details, please refer to the alternative channels.
 
 Single-name POSB/DBS/OCBC/UOB bank account under your Singapore NRIC 
 If you do not have a valid Singpass, but have a single-name POSB/DBS/OCBC/UOB bank account maintained under your Singapore NRIC number, please submit your application via this link.
 
 For more details, please refer to the alternative channels.
 
 In Singapore 
 Submit your application before leaving Singapore for faster processing. Once you have left the country, additional verification will be required for forms completed overseas as certification at a Singapore Overseas Mission is required. These may incur additional administrative charges.
 
 Please make an appointment to visit the CPF Service Centre to submit your application. Please note that no walk-ins are allowed without an appointment.
 
 When visiting the CPF Service Centre, please bring along your original copies of the supporting documents stated on the CPF account closure form.
 
 Not in Singapore 
 If you do not meet the conditions of all the application modes mentioned above, please submit your application form and required supporting documents by post via registered mail.
 
 Both the application form and all original supporting documents must be witnessed/certified true by an official of a Singapore Overseas Mission (SOM) with his official seal/stamp duly affixed. Please make an appointment in advance before you visit the SOM as walk-in applicants may not be served.
 
 Alternatively, if you are residing in a country which is part of the Apostille Convention, your documents must be:
 
 witnessed and certified true by a Notary Public; and then
 certified by the designated Competent Authority of your country via the issuance of an apostille certificate.
 Below age 21 Your parent or legal guardian will have to submit your application via this form, on your behalf.

CPF_DATA.q: What is a Singapore Overseas Mission?
CPF_DATA.a:
Singapore Overseas Missions located in certain countries can help CPF members residing overseas in witnessing and certification of application forms and documents.
 
 You will need to visit the Singapore Overseas Mission in person. Find out where the overseas mission nearest to you is.

CPF_DATA.q: How do I apply to be insured under the Home Protection Scheme (HPS) after I obtained Singapore Citizenship or Permanent Residency again?
CPF_DATA.a:
You may re-apply for a Home Protection Scheme cover online, using your Singpass, if your previous HPS cover has ceased and you are the owner of a HDB flat with an outstanding housing loan.
 
 Your eligibility for HPS coverage is subject to approval and your current health condition.

CPF_DATA.q: Do I need to refund the transferred CPF savings if I decide to obtain Singapore Citizenship or Permanent Residency again after I have closed my account?
Will I be able to return to Singapore for work if I have closed my CPF account?
CPF_DATA.a:
For enquiries on work permit or employment pass application, please contact Ministry of Manpower at 6438 5122 or send your enquiries to them using the online feedback service.
 
 For enquiries on applying for Permanent Residency in Singapore, you may submit your request via ICA | Feedback.

CPF_DATA.q: What is Central Provident Fund (CPF)?
CPF_DATA.a:
The Central Provident Fund (CPF) is a comprehensive social security system that enables working Singapore Citizens and Permanent Residents to set aside funds for retirement. It also addresses healthcare, home ownership, family protection and asset enhancement.
 
 Working Singaporeans and their employers make monthly contributions to the CPF and these contributions go into three accounts:
 
 Ordinary Account - the savings can be used to buy a home, pay for CPF insurance, investment and education.
 
 Special Account - for old age and investment in retirement-related financial products.
 
 MediSave Account - the savings can be used for hospitalisation expenses and approved medical insurance.
 
 Please refer to the CPF website for an overview of CPF.

CPF_DATA.q: What is the CPF website?
CPF_DATA.a: The CPF website (cpf.gov.sg) gives you quick access to information on CPF schemes and services, and the latest CPF news. Members can also perform CPF transactions such as accessing their CPF account information and transacting online through my cpf digital services.

CPF_DATA.q: I have issues with my Singpass. What should I do?
CPF_DATA.a:
Singpass is an authentication service governed by GovTech. CPF Board is one of the government agencies using Singpass and its two-factor authentication (2FA) service.
 
 If you have any Singpass issues (e.g. unable to login via Singpass, forgot Singpass password, unable to receive SMS OTP), please contact the Singpass Helpdesk directly.

CPF_DATA.q: How do I change or reset my Singpass password online?
CPF_DATA.a:
To change or reset your Singpass password, please complete the following steps:
 
 Visit Singpass website
 Select 'Log in'
 Select ‘Reset password’ (under the ‘Services’ tab)
 Follow the instructions on the website to complete the changing or resetting of Singpass password
 If you require further assistance, please contact the Singpass Helpdesk directly.

CPF_DATA.q: Why is Singpass Face Verification failing for me?
CPF_DATA.a:
There may be several possible reasons for this. For ways to improve the success of Singpass Face Verification, you should ensure that:
 
 Your face is not covered
 You keep still while scanning is in progress
 Your environment is not too dark
 There is no strong lighting in the background
 You use a minimum resolution of two megapixels for the camera. If you are using a laptop, you may wish to try again using a mobile phone which has a better camera
 If you require assistance with Singpass Face Verification, please contact the Singpass Helpdesk directly.

CPF_DATA.q: What is Singpass?
CPF_DATA.a: Singpass stands for Singapore Personal Access and it is your common password to transact with the Government online. Please visit Singpass website for more information on Singpass.

CPF_DATA.q: How do I get a Singpass?
CPF_DATA.a:
To obtain a Singpass, you can either:
 
 visit Singpass website to apply for your Singpass online or
 visit any of the Singpass counters located islandwide in-person.

CPF_DATA.q: Why am I unable to log in to CPF Mobile through the Singpass QR code?
CPF_DATA.a:
The Singpass app is required in order to log in to CPF Mobile using the Singpass QR code. Singpass app is compatible with the following mobile browsers:
 
 Safari versions: 12.1 and above
 Chrome versions: 78 above
 You may encounter login errors if you have set other browsers as your mobile device’s default browser. To ensure a seamless login experience, please set one of the browsers above as your mobile device’s default browser. Alternatively, you may log in without the Singpass app by selecting ‘Password login’ on the Singpass login page and logging in using your Singpass ID and password. If you continue to face issues pertaining to Singpass login, please contact the Singpass Helpdesk directly for further assistance.
 
 If you are experiencing other login issues, please refer to our FAQ on troubleshooting for more information.

CPF_DATA.q: I am a foreigner. I can't seem to be able to login to CPF digital services using my Singpass, why is it so?
CPF_DATA.a:
With the discontinuation of the OneKey Token from 1 April 2021, foreign CPF members will no longer be able to access CPF digital services using the Singpass which was registered under their CPF issued account numbers (e.g. SA987654C or TC123456A) previously.
 
 Foreign CPF members with CPF issued accounts and are currently holding FIN may apply for a new Singpass under the FIN to access CPF digital services for their CPF SA/SB/TC account. You may refer to the Singpass website at singpass.gov.sg for the steps to apply.
 
 CPF members who do not meet the above criteria may Write to Us for assistance.

CPF_DATA.q: How do I set up Singpass Face Verification (SFV)?
CPF_DATA.a:
Singpass Face Verification (SFV) does not require any setup. It will automatically be triggered for potentially suspicious or higher-risk logins and transactions.
 
 Please contact the Singpass Helpdesk directly for more information on Singpass Face Verification.

CPF_DATA.q: Why am I prompted for Singpass Face Verification when performing transactions using my cpf digital services?
CPF_DATA.a:
This is an additional safeguard against unauthorised usage of your Singpass account on transactions that require Singpass.
 
 Please contact the Singpass Helpdesk directly for more information on Singpass Face Verification.

CPF_DATA.q: What should I do if I face difficulty logging into my cpf digital services?
CPF_DATA.a:
Here are some ways for you to troubleshoot when encountering login issues:
 
 Check your internet connection to ensure it is stable.
 Clear your browser cache before trying again (please refer to this link for instructions on how to clear the cache for all major browsers).
 Relaunch CPF website and try to log in again.
 Check that your browser is updated. my cpf digital services is compatible with the two most recent versions of commonly used web browsers such as Chrome and Safari.
 Check that your device date-time setting is set to “Automatic” or the current date and time
 Try logging in to other government websites to determine if the issue occurs on these other platforms.
 If the issue persists, please write to us for further assistance.

CPF_DATA.q: How can I download CPF Mobile?
CPF_DATA.a: You can download CPF Mobile directly to your mobile phone from either the Apple App Store or the Google Play Store. Please ensure that the developer of the application is Central Provident Fund Board.

CPF_DATA.q: What is CPF Mobile?
CPF_DATA.a: CPF Mobile provides you with easy access to your personal CPF information on the go and quick access to your CPF statement and services to manage your CPF. You can also receive push notification alerts and updates regarding your personal CPF matters, educational articles, CPF tips and events even when you are on the move. For more information, please see CPF Mobile services. It is available in both the Apple App Store and Google Play Store.

CPF_DATA.q: What is the Silver Support Scheme?
CPF_DATA.a:
The Silver Support (SS) Scheme is part of a wider suite of schemes (e.g. healthcare subsidies, GST voucher) that the Government has put in place to give seniors greater assurance in retirement.
  
 It provides a quarterly cash supplement to seniors who had low incomes during their working years and now have less in retirement. The Government reviews the Scheme periodically to ensure that it continues to target deserving seniors.
  
 Seniors who qualify for SS will automatically receive their cash supplement. No application is required.
  
 To check your eligibility status or submit an enquiry, log in to e-services.

CPF_DATA.q: Do I need to apply for the Silver Support Scheme?
CPF_DATA.a:
There is no need to apply for the Silver Support (SS) Scheme.
  
 CPF Board will automatically review your eligibility for the SS Scheme every year. Eligible seniors will automatically receive SS every quarter.
  
 Seniors who qualify will receive a notification from CPF Board in December of the preceding year.

CPF_DATA.q: Do I qualify for the Silver Support Scheme?
CPF_DATA.a:
The Silver Support (SS) Scheme is meant to support seniors who had low incomes during their working years and now have less in retirement. To identify this target group, three criteria are used in tandem – lifetime wages, housing type, and the level of household support.
  
 Singapore citizens aged 65 and above will be eligible for the SS Scheme if they meet all of the following criteria:
  
 1. Low lifetime wages - Total CPF contributions by age 55 is not more than $140,000. This refers to the total sum in an individual’s Ordinary Account and Special Account, including the amounts withdrawn for housing, education and investment.
  
 Self-employed persons and platform workers should also have an average annual net trade income of not more than $27,600 when they were between the ages of 45 and 54. Net trade income refers to the gross trade income minus all allowable business expenses, capital allowances and trade losses as determined by IRAS; and
  
 2. Housing type - Live in a 1- to 5-room HDB flat; and do not own, or have a spouse who owns, a 5-room or larger HDB flat, private property or multiple properties; and
  
 3. Low household support - Live in a household with a monthly income per person of not more than $2,300 (was $1,800 in year 2024).
  
 CPF Board will automatically review your eligibility for SS every year. Eligible seniors turning age 65 will start receiving SS in the quarter they turn 65. Seniors who qualify for SS will receive a notification in December of the preceding year.
  
 In addition to the notification, all Singaporeans aged 65 and above can check their SS eligibility by logging in to the SS e-services using their Singpass.

CPF_DATA.q: How much Silver Support will I receive?
CPF_DATA.a:
Eligible seniors will receive Silver Support (SS) every quarter, depending on their household monthly income per person, and the type of HDB flat that they live in. Those living in smaller flats will receive higher SS. Seniors who are ComCare Long Term Assistance (LTA) recipients will receive SS of $430 per quarter, regardless of their flat type.

 3. Senior may live in, but do not own, a 5-room HDB flat.
