CPF_DATA.q: What happens to the Silver Support paid via GovCash if the beneficiary passes away?
CPF_DATA.a: If the beneficiary passes away before fully using their GovCash, the remaining balance will be credited to their CPF Ordinary Account. This amount will form part of their CPF monies and will be distributed according to their CPF Nomination, if any.

CPF_DATA.q: When should I receive my CPF contributions?
CPF_DATA.a:
Your employer must pay your CPF contributions for each month's wages by the 14th of the following month (or the next working day if the 14th falls on a Saturday, Sunday or Public Holiday). If your employer pays after the 14th, they may incur late payment interest charged at 1.5% per month.
 
 Once CPFB has received the payment, the contributions will be credited to your CPF accounts within three working days, and you will be able to view the amounts through CPF transaction history using your Singpass.
 
 If you notice that your CPF contributions are not paid accurately or timely, you should check with your employer immediately.

CPF_DATA.q: What should I do if my employer did not pay my CPF contributions on time?
CPF_DATA.a:
If you are not paid your CPF contributions on time, you should approach your employer for an explanation and ask when your employer will be making the payment.
 
 If your employer does not provide you with a satisfactory response, you can lodge a report with CPF Board.
 
 The Board takes a serious view of employers who default on CPF contributions. The due date for CPF contributions is on the last day of the calendar month. Enforcement action would be taken against employers who fail to pay by the 14th of the following month (or the next working day if the 14th falls on a Saturday, Sunday or Public Holiday). This includes imposing late payment interest charged at 1.5% per month commencing from the first day after the due date. In cases where the company is in financial difficulty, CPF arrears may not always be recoverable despite our enforcement efforts.
 
 You may find out more on your rights and employer’s obligations under the CPF Act and the Employment Act.
 
 If you face difficulty servicing your Housing Development Board (HDB) loans, you may contact HDB to explore possible alternative housing repayment options. In the event that you wish to look for alternative employment, you may approach Workforce Singapore for assistance.

CPF_DATA.q: How do I view, save or download my Transaction History or my CPF contribution details, including records beyond 15 months?
CPF_DATA.a:
You can access your CPF Transaction History, through CPF Mobile app or my cpf digital services.
 
 CPF Transaction History provides a record of your CPF transactions which includes contributions, withdrawals, transfers, etc. for the latest 15 months.
 
 You can customise your view using the filter option to display your CPF contributions, such as:
 Employment and Platform Contributions
 Self-Employed Contributions
 To view and save your statement, please refer to this guide (PDF, 0.28MB). To find your downloaded documents on mobile devices, refer to this FAQ.
 
 For records beyond 15 months, you can refer to your Yearly Statement of Account, which covers up to the past 10 years.
 
 Please note that we do not provide the service of retrieving CPF statements beyond the past 10 years. It is your responsibility to carefully review your CPF statements and download your statements in a timely manner to ensure that you always have a copy for your own records.
 
 If you do not have a Singpass and/or 2FA, please visit the Singpass website to obtain one.

CPF_DATA.q: How do I view, save or download my monthly transaction statement?
CPF_DATA.a:
Members can now view their monthly transactions in 'Transaction history' under ‘My statements'.
 
 Upon receiving your monthly email notification, follow these steps to view, save and download your statement:
 
 1. Click the "View now" button in the email and you will be redirected to the Singpass login page.
 2. Log in with your Singpass to authenticate your identity.
 3. Once authenticated, your CPF Transaction history statement will be displayed. To obtain a statement for a specific month, enter the same month for both the start and end month in the Transaction history (e.g. to generate the monthly statement for Jan 2026, select “Jan 2026" for both the start and end month). For detailed guidance, please refer to our step-by-step guide below.
 4. To save or download your monthly transaction statement, click the PDF icon on the top right corner. To find your downloaded documents on mobile devices, refer to this FAQ.

CPF_DATA.q: What should I do if I think the information in my CPF statement is incorrect?
CPF_DATA.a: Please notify us immediately via Write to Us if you think that the information is incorrect. Please note that it is your responsibility to carefully review your CPF statements.

CPF_DATA.q: Why did CPF Board send me a notification to view my transactions? Is it legitimate?
CPF_DATA.a:
Monthly notifications are sent to members with registered email addresses, reminding them to review their transactions every month. This ensures members can promptly identify and report any discrepancies.
 
 To verify the legitimacy of the notification, members should:
 
 1. Ensure that the sender’s email address is correctly spelt and ends with @e.cpf.gov.sg.
 2. Ensure that the hyperlinks or links they click on has a domain ending with “gov.sg”. 
 
 To ensure your email address is up-to-date, you can check or update it using your Singpass.
 
 Protect yourself from phishing: Learn how to identify phishing links and safeguard your account.

CPF_DATA.q: Can I view my child's CPF statements online?
CPF_DATA.a:
You can view your child’s Transaction History statement as well as the Healthcare Payments and Claims statement (if applicable) for the last 15 months online if:
 
 1. You and your child are Singapore Citizens or Permanent Residents, and
 
 2. Your child is below the age of 21 and has received CPF contributions (including Government Grants such as MediSave Grant for Newborns).
 
 Simply login to my cpf Home > Child Dashboard with your Singpass to access this information.
 
 If you do not see your child’s name listed in the Child Dashboard, you can write to us with the following supporting documents and we will be happy to assist.
 
 Supporting documents required:
 Birth certificate of your child
 Citizenship certificate if your child was a foreigner at birth (where applicable).

CPF_DATA.q: If I am granted sole custody of my child following a divorce, can I request to remove my ex-spouse's access to our child's CPF statement?
CPF_DATA.a: Yes, you can write to us using your Singpass and attach the Court Order as a supporting document.

CPF_DATA.q: Can I make further changes to my contact details while my current request is still in the 12-hour cooling period?
CPF_DATA.a:
Yes, you can submit further changes to your contact details via the Account settings page with your Singpass.
 
 Please note that your most recent request will supersede the previous submission and the changes will take effect after the 12-hour cooling period.

CPF_DATA.q: How do I update my registered bank account details with CPF Board?
CPF_DATA.a:
You can update your registered bank account details with CPF Board to receive payments such as withdrawal of CPF savings and monthly payouts via my cpf digital services - Account settings with your Singpass.
 
 You may need to verify your identity using enhanced authentication measures to update your registered bank account details.
 
 To guide you on how to update your registered bank account details with CPF Board, watch the instructional video in your preferred language, or follow the steps below:
 
 Log in to your CPF account with your Singpass.
 On the website menu, click on ‘Account services’ > ‘Your account information’ > ‘Update account settings’.
 Click the ‘Bank account’ tab and click ‘Update’.
 For PayNow, select ‘PayNow NRIC-linked bank account’ and submit the form. The bank account will be updated after 12 hours
 
 For Interbank GIRO, key in your bank account details and submit the form. The bank account will generally take up to two working days to be activated after your bank confirms that the bank account belongs to you.

CPF_DATA.q: I have updated my email address/mobile number with Singpass. Why are my Singpass contact details not updated with CPF Board?
CPF_DATA.a:
Updates to your Singpass mobile number and email address will not be automatically updated with the Board.
 
 You can update your mobile number and/or email address via my cpf digital services – Account settings with your Singpass. An SMS or email One-Time PIN (OTP) will be sent to your new mobile or email address for verification before the changes can be submitted.
 
 Please note that any changes to your contact details will be subject to a 12-hour cooling period and you may be subject to enhanced authentication measures to verify your identity.
 
 You can also watch the instructional video on how to update your contact details with CPF Board.

CPF_DATA.q: How do I inform CPF Board if I have changed my contact details?
CPF_DATA.a:
Notifications will be sent to you whenever you make a transaction. Also, we may contact you to perform further verification checks on your transactions for added security. Hence, it is important to check and keep your contact details updated.
 
 You can update your mobile number and/or email address via the Account Settings page with your Singpass. An SMS or email One-Time Password (OTP) will be sent to your new mobile or email address for verification before the changes can be submitted.
 
 Please note that any changes to your contact details will be subject to a 12-hour cooling period and you may be subject to enhanced authentication measures to verify your identity.
 
 You can also watch the instructional video below on how to update your contact details with CPF Board.

CPF_DATA.q: What is the Trusted Contact notification service?
CPF_DATA.a:
Trusted Contact notification service is a digital service that allows you to appoint a trusted individual, such as a family member to receive a copy of your notifications whenever important transactions and updates occur in your CPF account.
 
 Please refer to the list of notifications that are copied to your Trusted Contact to see what information they can view.

CPF_DATA.q: Why should I appoint a Trusted Contact?
CPF_DATA.a:
Anyone can fall prey to scams or have their personal devices compromised by malicious software. When life gets busy, it becomes easy to overlook suspicious interactions.
 
 By receiving a copy of your transaction notifications, your Trusted Contact provides an additional layer of scrutiny for your CPF transactions. Your Trusted Contact can spot unusual or unexpected activities that you might miss and alert you promptly, allowing for early intervention to potentially mitigate losses.
 
 Please refer to the list of notifications that are copied to your Trusted Contact to see what information they can view.

CPF_DATA.q: What can my Trusted Contact view or do?
CPF_DATA.a:
What can my Trusted Contact view or do?
 Your appointed Trusted Contact will receive a copy of the notifications sent to you whenever you perform any of the following transactions and updates in your CPF account. Their role is limited to receiving a copy of your transaction notifications and alerting you when they notice any unusual or unexpected transactions or updates made in your CPF account.
 
 Notifications from the following CPFB services are copied to your Trusted Contact:
 CPF lump sum withdrawals for immediate retirement needs
 Contact details update
 Bank account update
 Daily withdrawal limit update
 You will continue to receive these notifications at your own registered email address and mobile number.
 
 Please note that your Trusted Contact cannot view your CPF account details beyond the information included in these notifications, make transactions, or change any account settings on your behalf.

CPF_DATA.q: What information does my Trusted Contact see in the notifications copied to them?
CPF_DATA.a:
Successfully appointed Trusted Contacts receive copies of the same notifications that are sent to you when transactions are made. The information in these notifications will differ for each CPF service used and transaction made.
 
 You cannot customise the notifications or information sent to your Trusted Contacts. They will receive copies of the same notifications you receive to ensure they can effectively detect any unusual or unexpected activities in your CPF account. We recommend appointing someone you trust to help monitor important CPF transactions as an additional layer of security against potential scams.
 
 Information included in the notifications:
 
 CPF service Information included 
 CPF lump sum withdrawals for
 immediate retirement needs
 Transaction date, withdrawal amount, details of Bank Account used for withdrawal, reason(s) for unsuccessful withdrawal (where applicable)
 Contact details update
 Transaction date, Transaction time
 Bank account update
 Transaction number, Transaction date, Transaction time, Update option (PayNow or Interbank GIRO), details of new Bank Account
 Daily withdrawal limit update
 Transaction date, previous Daily Withdrawal Limit, updated Daily Withdrawal Limit
 
 You can also view a sample of the notification content.

CPF_DATA.q: Why is my appointed Trusted Contact not allowed to view my CPF account balances / statements? Why are they not allowed to transact using my CPF account?
CPF_DATA.a:
As the account holder, you maintain complete control over your CPF account. This is of critical importance as your CPF account holds your retirement savings and payout arrangements.
 
 Your Trusted Contact can only receive key transaction information through notifications to help you monitor for suspicious transactions - they cannot view your full CPF account details, make transactions, or change any account settings on your behalf. This ensures your Trusted Contact can help identify suspicious transactions while your account privacy remains protected.

CPF_DATA.q: How long does a Trusted Contact appointment remain valid for?
CPF_DATA.a:
Trusted Contact appointments will remain valid until either you or your appointed Trusted Contact terminates the arrangement. The CPF Board will also remove Trusted Contact appointments when either party can no longer use the service, such as upon demise.
 
 Do note that Trusted Contact(s) you have appointed will not be affected and remains valid even after you lose mental capacity.

CPF_DATA.q: Will my Trusted Contact(s) be held accountable if they fail to notify me about the unusual or unexpected transactions in my CPF account?
CPF_DATA.a:
Although your Trusted Contact(s) will receive a copy of your CPF transaction notifications, they cannot be held accountable if they do not alert you to unusual or unexpected activities. You remain responsible for all activities which take place through the use of your Singpass, and for managing your CPF account. 
 
 Apart from appointing a Trusted Contact, the CPF Board strongly encourages you to update your contact details so that you can be notified when transactions occur in your CPF account. You can also download CPF Mobile on your mobile device and turn on push notifications to receive alerts on transactions performed in your CPF account.

CPF_DATA.q: What happens to my Trusted Contact appointment(s) if I lose my mental capacity?
CPF_DATA.a: Your Trusted Contact appointment will not be affected and remains valid even after you lose mental capacity. The CPF Board will uphold any decisions made by members prior to the loss of mental capacity.

CPF_DATA.q: Can I appoint a new Trusted Contact or remove existing Trusted Contact on someone’s behalf?
CPF_DATA.a:
No, Trusted Contact appointments or removal of existing Trusted Contact appointments must be made by the CPF member personally. 
 
 However, if the CPF member loses their mental capacity, their Deputy or Donee may apply to remove existing Trusted Contacts on their behalf.

CPF_DATA.q: I am providing caregiving to a loved one with medical conditions. I would like to make changes to their Trusted Contact appointment(s). How can I do so?
CPF_DATA.a:
Trusted Contact appointments or removal of existing Trusted Contacts must be made by the CPF member personally. Your loved one can make changes to their Trusted Contact appointment online via the Add or remove Trusted Contact e-form.
 
 As a third party, you cannot make decisions concerning another member’s CPF account unless you have been legally authorised to do so.
 
 If your loved one has lost mental capacity and you are their Deputy or Donee, you can write to us via Singpass to request the removal of appointed Trusted Contacts on their behalf, by providing the following documents:
 For Donee appointed under a Lasting Power of Attorney (LPA): Submit the LPA document and a medical report certifying your next-of-kin’s loss of mental capacity
 For Deputy appointed by the Singapore Court: Submit the Deputyship court order
 If your loved one has mental capacity but you wish to act on their behalf, you will need to write to us via Singpass and furnish a court order authorising you to make such changes.

CPF_DATA.q: How do I appoint a Trusted Contact?
CPF_DATA.a:
You can easily appoint a Trusted Contact via the Add or remove Trusted Contact e-form. You will need to log in with your Singpass to access the e-form.
 
 Before you proceed:
 Verify that your contact details registered with CPF Board are correct. If you need to update your contact details, you can do so via Account settings before returning to this form.
 
 Steps to appoint:
 1. Select the ‘Add a Trusted Contact’ option at Step 1 of the form.
 
 2. Provide the following details of the person you wish to appoint:
 a. Full name (as per NRIC) 
 b. NRIC number 
 c. Relationship to you 
 
 3. Review your input before submitting the e-form.
 
 What happens next:
 Both you and your Trusted Contact appointee will be notified upon a successful submission.
 The Trusted Contact appointment will only take effect once your appointee accepts the request.
 Once your request has been accepted, your Trusted Contact will receive a copy of your notifications whenever important transactions occur in your CPF account.
 Important notes:
 You may need to verify your identity using enhanced authentication measures to complete the submission.
 Your appointee has 7 days to respond to your Trusted Contact appointment request, after which the request will automatically expire.
 If the request has expired, you will need to submit a new appointment request. We strongly encourage you to personally inform your appointee about the appointment request to ensure they are aware of the appointment request and understand their role as a Trusted Contact.

CPF_DATA.q: How do I update my registered bank account details with CPF Board?
CPF_DATA.a:
You can update your registered bank account details with CPF Board to receive payments such as withdrawal of CPF savings and monthly payouts via my cpf digital services - Account settings with your Singpass.
 
 You may need to verify your identity using enhanced authentication measures to update your registered bank account details.
 
 To guide you on how to update your registered bank account details with CPF Board, watch the instructional video in your preferred language, or follow the steps below:
 
 Log in to your CPF account with your Singpass.
 On the website menu, click on ‘Account services’ > ‘Your account information’ > ‘Update account settings’.
 Click the ‘Bank account’ tab and click ‘Update’.
 For PayNow, select ‘PayNow NRIC-linked bank account’ and submit the form. The bank account will be updated after 12 hours
 
 For Interbank GIRO, key in your bank account details and submit the form. The bank account will generally take up to two working days to be activated after your bank confirms that the bank account belongs to you

CPF_DATA.q: When do I need to update my bank account?
CPF_DATA.a:
You should review and update your bank account details when there are any changes to avoid any delay or disruption in receiving your payments. If you have a pending withdrawal, consider updating your bank account after the withdrawal is complete, to enable successful processing of your withdrawal application.
 
 Your monthly payouts and CPF savings withdrawn (see list of payments) will be paid to the bank account you have provided under your Account settings.
 
 You can update your bank account details via my cpf digital services – Account settings with your Singpass.

CPF_DATA.q: Why do I need to update contact details to update my bank account?
CPF_DATA.a: Notifications via SMS or email will be sent to you whenever there are updates to your bank account. As such, you will have to register or update your contact details under Account settings to receive these notifications and prevent any unauthorised updates from taking effect.

CPF_DATA.q: How early can I make an appointment at the Service Centre?
CPF_DATA.a: Service Centre appointment bookings are opened 2 weeks in advance.

CPF_DATA.q: Why am I unable to make an appointment at the Service Centre more than 2 weeks in advance?
CPF_DATA.a:
Based on our experience, the 2-week advance booking window meets the needs of most CPF members. Booking appointments too far in advance may lead to missed appointments when there are changes in members’ circumstances or needs.
 
 With a 2-week booking window, we are better able to ensure that our customer service officers are accessible to members who need assistance.

CPF_DATA.q: Where are the CPF Service Centres located?
CPF_DATA.a:
You may visit us at any of the Service Centre locations.
 
 Services for employer or self-employed matters are only available through our digital services, hotline or Write to us.
 
 For CPF related matters or assistance, you can make an appointment to visit any of our Service Centres or to speak with our Customer Service Executive over the phone.

CPF_DATA.q: What is the CPF website?
CPF_DATA.a: The CPF website (cpf.gov.sg) gives you quick access to information on CPF schemes and services, and the latest CPF news. Members can also perform CPF transactions such as accessing their CPF account information and transacting online through my cpf digital services.

CPF_DATA.q: How do I make an appointment with CPF?
CPF_DATA.a:
Services for employer or self-employed matters are only available through our digital services, hotline or Write to us.
 
 For assistance on CPF-related matters, you can make an appointment to visit any of our Service Centres or to speak with our Customer Service Executive over the phone.
 
 Please note that counter services are available by appointment only.

CPF_DATA.q: I have a CPF account and Singpass. How do I set up my Singpass Two-Factor Authentication (2FA)?
CPF_DATA.a: To set up your Singpass 2FA, please follow the steps in the instructional guide

CPF_DATA.q: How do I change or reset my Singpass password online?
CPF_DATA.a:
To change or reset your Singpass password, please complete the following steps:
 
 Visit Singpass website
 Select 'Log in'
 Select ‘Reset password’ (under the ‘Services’ tab)
 Follow the instructions on the website to complete the changing or resetting of Singpass password
 If you require further assistance, please contact the Singpass Helpdesk directly.

CPF_DATA.q: I have renounced my Singapore Citizenship and I can no longer use my Singpass to access my CPF Statement. How do I access my CPF Statement?
CPF_DATA.a:
You may write to us for CPF-related assistance.
 
 Please contact the Singpass Helpdesk directly to find out the groups of users eligible to apply for Singpass.

CPF_DATA.q: How do I check/change/cancel my appointment?
CPF_DATA.a: If you have made an appointment with our Customer Service Executive and need to check/reschedule/cancel the session, you can do so at Book an appointment.

CPF_DATA.q: I am a FIN holder. How do I access CPF digital services?
CPF_DATA.a:
You may login to CPF digital services using your Singpass registered under your FIN.
 
 If you have a CPF issued account (e.g. SA987654C or TC123456A) and your FIN is updated with us, you will be prompted to select your transaction profile (either CPF issued account or FIN) after you log in.

CPF_DATA.q: What is Singpass Face Verification?
CPF_DATA.a:
Singpass Face Verification is a method of verifying your identity using a face scan compared against the government's records.
 
 Please contact the Singpass Helpdesk directly for more information on Singpass Face Verification.

CPF_DATA.q: I am a foreigner. I can't seem to be able to login to CPF digital services using my Singpass, why is it so?
CPF_DATA.a:
With the discontinuation of the OneKey Token from 1 April 2021, foreign CPF members will no longer be able to access CPF digital services using the Singpass which was registered under their CPF issued account numbers (e.g. SA987654C or TC123456A) previously.
 
 Foreign CPF members with CPF issued accounts and are currently holding FIN may apply for a new Singpass under the FIN to access CPF digital services for their CPF SA/SB/TC account. You may refer to the Singpass website at singpass.gov.sg for the steps to apply.
 
 CPF members who do not meet the above criteria may Write to Us for assistance.

CPF_DATA.q: What is the Trusted Contact notification service?
CPF_DATA.a:
Trusted Contact notification service is a digital service that allows you to appoint a trusted individual, such as a family member to receive a copy of your notifications whenever important transactions and updates occur in your CPF account.
 
 Please refer to the list of notifications that are copied to your Trusted Contact to see what information they can view.

CPF_DATA.q: Why should I appoint a Trusted Contact?
CPF_DATA.a:
Anyone can fall prey to scams or have their personal devices compromised by malicious software. When life gets busy, it becomes easy to overlook suspicious interactions.
  
 By receiving a copy of your transaction notifications, your Trusted Contact provides an additional layer of scrutiny for your CPF transactions. Your Trusted Contact can spot unusual or unexpected activities that you might miss and alert you promptly, allowing for early intervention to potentially mitigate losses.
  
 Please refer to the list of notifications that are copied to your Trusted Contact to see what information they can view.

CPF_DATA.q: What can my Trusted Contact view or do?
CPF_DATA.a:
Your appointed Trusted Contact will receive a copy of the notifications sent to you whenever you perform any of the following transactions and updates in your CPF account. Their role is limited to receiving a copy of your transaction notifications and alerting you when they notice any unusual or unexpected transactions or updates made in your CPF account.
  
 Notifications from the following CPFB services are copied to your Trusted Contact:
 CPF lump sum withdrawals for immediate retirement needs
 Contact details update
 Bank account update
 Daily withdrawal limit update
 You will continue to receive these notifications at your own registered email address and mobile number.
  
 Please note that your Trusted Contact cannot view your CPF account details beyond the information included in these notifications, make transactions, or change any account settings on your behalf.

CPF_DATA.q: What information does my Trusted Contact see in the notifications copied to them?
CPF_DATA.a:
Successfully appointed Trusted Contacts receive copies of the same notifications that are sent to you when transactions are made. The information in these notifications will differ for each CPF service used and transaction made.
 
 You cannot customise the notifications or information sent to your Trusted Contacts. They will receive copies of the same notifications you receive to ensure they can effectively detect any unusual or unexpected activities in your CPF account. We recommend appointing someone you trust to help monitor important CPF transactions as an additional layer of security against potential scams.
 
 Information included in the notifications:
 
 CPF service Information included 
 CPF lump sum withdrawals for
 immediate retirement needs
 Transaction date, withdrawal amount, details of Bank Account used for withdrawal, reason(s) for unsuccessful withdrawal (where applicable)
 Contact details update
 Transaction date, Transaction time
 Bank account update
 Transaction number, Transaction date, Transaction time, Update option (PayNow or Interbank GIRO), details of new Bank Account
 Daily withdrawal limit update
 Transaction date, previous Daily Withdrawal Limit, updated Daily Withdrawal Limit
 
 You can also view a sample of the notification content.

CPF_DATA.q: Why is my appointed Trusted Contact not allowed to view my CPF account balances / statements? Why are they not allowed to transact using my CPF account?
CPF_DATA.a:
As the account holder, you maintain complete control over your CPF account. This is of critical importance as your CPF account holds your retirement savings and payout arrangements.
 
 Your Trusted Contact can only receive key transaction information through notifications to help you monitor for suspicious transactions - they cannot view your full CPF account details, make transactions, or change any account settings on your behalf. This ensures your Trusted Contact can help identify suspicious transactions while your account privacy remains protected.

CPF_DATA.q: How long does a Trusted Contact appointment remain valid for?
CPF_DATA.a:
Trusted Contact appointments will remain valid until either you or your appointed Trusted Contact terminates the arrangement. The CPF Board will also remove Trusted Contact appointments when either party can no longer use the service, such as upon demise.
  
 Do note that Trusted Contact(s) you have appointed will not be affected and remains valid even after you lose mental capacity.

CPF_DATA.q: Will my Trusted Contact(s) be held accountable if they fail to notify me about the unusual or unexpected transactions in my CPF account?
CPF_DATA.a:
Although your Trusted Contact(s) will receive a copy of your CPF transaction notifications, they cannot be held accountable if they do not alert you to unusual or unexpected activities. You remain responsible for all activities which take place through the use of your Singpass, and for managing your CPF account. 
  
 Apart from appointing a Trusted Contact, the CPF Board strongly encourages you to update your contact details so that you can be notified when transactions occur in your CPF account. You can also download CPF Mobile on your mobile device and turn on push notifications to receive alerts on transactions performed in your CPF account.

CPF_DATA.q: How is the Trusted Contact notification service different from CPF nomination?
CPF_DATA.a:
The Trusted Contact notification service and CPF nomination are two separate CPF services:
  
 The Trusted Contact notification service: Allows you to appoint a trusted individual such as a family member, to receive a copy of your notifications whenever important transactions occur in your CPF account.
 A CPF nomination allows you to specify who will receive your CPF savings and the proportion of your savings each nominee will receive when you pass away.
 Appointing a Trusted Contact does not affect your CPF nomination. Likewise, making a CPF nomination does not affect your Trusted Contact appointment(s) too. If you wish, you may appoint the same individual as both your nominee and your Trusted Contact.

CPF_DATA.q: How is the Trusted Contact different from a Court-appointed Deputyship?
CPF_DATA.a:
A Deputy is an individual appointed by the court to make decisions on behalf of a person who lacks mental capacity. The type of decisions a Deputy can make are specified in the Deputyship appointment court order.
  
 In contrast, a Trusted Contact is a trusted person that a CPF member can appoint, while they have mental capacity, to receive a copy of their notifications whenever important transactions occur in their CPF account. Unlike a Deputy, a Trusted Contact is not legally empowered to make decisions on the person’s behalf.

CPF_DATA.q: How is the Trusted Contact different from a Lasting Power of Attorney (LPA)?
CPF_DATA.a:
An Lasting Power of Attorney (LPA) allows a person to appoint someone (Donee) to make decisions on their behalf, should they lose mental capacity in the future. A Donee can be appointed to act in the two broad areas of personal welfare and/or property and affairs matters. The LPA also gives the authority to the Donee to sign deeds on the Donor's behalf.
  
 While both Donee and Trusted Contact appointments are initiated by the CPF member, unlike a Donee, a Trusted Contact is not legally empowered to make decisions on the person’s behalf. The Trusted Contact can only receive a copy of their appointer’s notifications whenever important transactions occur in their CPF account. 
 
 You should also consider appointing an LPA as part of your legacy planning if you have not done so already.

CPF_DATA.q: What are the Trusted Contact notification service Terms and Conditions?
CPF_DATA.a:
You may refer to the terms and conditions for the Trusted Contact notification service below: 
 Terms and Conditions.

CPF_DATA.q: What happens to my Trusted Contact appointment(s) if I lose my mental capacity?
CPF_DATA.a: Your Trusted Contact appointment will not be affected and remains valid even after you lose mental capacity. The CPF Board will uphold any decisions made by members prior to the loss of mental capacity.

CPF_DATA.q: Can I appoint a new Trusted Contact or remove existing Trusted Contact on someone’s behalf?
CPF_DATA.a:
No, Trusted Contact appointments or removal of existing Trusted Contact appointments must be made by the CPF member personally. 
 
 However, if the CPF member loses their mental capacity, their Deputy or Donee may apply to remove existing Trusted Contacts on their behalf.

CPF_DATA.q: I am providing caregiving to a loved one with medical conditions. I would like to make changes to their Trusted Contact appointment(s). How can I do so?
CPF_DATA.a:
Trusted Contact appointments or removal of existing Trusted Contacts must be made by the CPF member personally. Your loved one can make changes to their Trusted Contact appointment online via the Add or remove Trusted Contact e-form.
 
 As a third party, you cannot make decisions concerning another member’s CPF account unless you have been legally authorised to do so.
 
 If your loved one has lost mental capacity and you are their Deputy or Donee, you can write to us via Singpass to request the removal of appointed Trusted Contacts on their behalf, by providing the following documents:
 For Donee appointed under a Lasting Power of Attorney (LPA): Submit the LPA document and a medical report certifying your next-of-kin’s loss of mental capacity
 For Deputy appointed by the Singapore Court: Submit the Deputyship court order
 If your loved one has mental capacity but you wish to act on their behalf, you will need to write to us via Singpass and furnish a court order authorising you to make such changes.

CPF_DATA.q: How do I appoint a Trusted Contact?
CPF_DATA.a:
You can easily appoint a Trusted Contact via the Add or remove Trusted Contact e-form. You will need to log in with your Singpass to access the e-form.
  
 Before you proceed:
 Verify that your contact details registered with CPF Board are correct. If you need to update your contact details, you can do so via Account settings before returning to this form.
  
 Steps to appoint:
 1. Select the ‘Add a Trusted Contact’ option at Step 1 of the form.
  
 2. Provide the following details of the person you wish to appoint:
 a. Full name (as per NRIC) 
 b. NRIC number 
 c. Relationship to you 
  
 3. Review your input before submitting the e-form.
  
 What happens next:
 Both you and your Trusted Contact appointee will be notified upon a successful submission.
 The Trusted Contact appointment will only take effect once your appointee accepts the request.
 Once your request has been accepted, your Trusted Contact will receive a copy of your notifications whenever important transactions occur in your CPF account.
 Important notes:
 You may need to verify your identity using enhanced authentication measures to complete the submission.
 Your appointee has 7 days to respond to your Trusted Contact appointment request, after which the request will automatically expire.
 If the request has expired, you will need to submit a new appointment request. We strongly encourage you to personally inform your appointee about the appointment request to ensure they are aware of the appointment request and understand their role as a Trusted Contact.

CPF_DATA.q: Who can appoint a Trusted Contact?
CPF_DATA.a:
You can appoint a Trusted Contact if you are: 
 
 A CPF member aged 21 and above; and 
 A Singapore Citizen or Singapore Permanent Resident (with Singpass account); and 
 Have at least a Singapore-registered mobile number or email address registered with CPF Board.

CPF_DATA.q: Why do I need to maintain contact details to use the Trusted Contact notification service?
CPF_DATA.a:
You will receive SMS and email notifications whenever there are changes to the status of your Trusted Contact appointment. To ensure you receive these alerts and can detect any unauthorised modifications, you need to register or update your contact details under Account settings. 
  
 Keeping your contact details up to date is crucial for your account security, as it ensures you receive timely notifications for your account activities. Regularly reviewing your contact details, especially after changing phone numbers or email addresses, helps maintain your account security.

CPF_DATA.q: Who can I appoint as my Trusted Contact?
CPF_DATA.a:
You should only appoint someone you trust, such as a family member, to help monitor important CPF transactions as an additional layer of security against potential scams.
  
 Your Trusted Contact must: 
 Be a CPF member aged 21 and above; 
 Be a Singapore Citizen or Singapore Permanent Resident (with Singpass account); and 
 Have both a Singapore-registered mobile number and email address registered with CPF Board.

CPF_DATA.q: Is there a limit to the number of Trusted Contacts I can appoint? How many Trusted Contacts can I appoint?
CPF_DATA.a: You may appoint up to two individuals as your Trusted Contacts. Both Trusted Contacts will have the same role and responsibilities with no primary or secondary designation. If you wish to appoint a new Trusted Contact when you have already reached the limit, you must first remove one of your existing Trusted Contacts.

CPF_DATA.q: Why am I not allowed to appoint more than two Trusted Contacts?
CPF_DATA.a: Your Trusted Contacts help to protect your CPF account by helping to detect any unexpected activity in your CPF account. The two-person limit is designed to provide optimal protection from potential scams while keeping your CPF information within a close, trusted circle. We encourage you to carefully select your most reliable and trusted contacts, ensuring focused and effective monitoring of your CPF transactions for suspicious activities.

CPF_DATA.q: Can I appoint someone who is not my next-of-kin to be my Trusted Contact?
CPF_DATA.a:
You can appoint a person who is not your next-of-kin to be your Trusted Contact. We highly recommend that you carefully review and only choose someone you trust, as they will help monitor important CPF transactions and provide an additional layer of security against potential scams.
 
 Do note that your appointee will also need to accept the appointment before they become your Trusted Contact.

CPF_DATA.q: Can I appoint a Trusted Contact who is not based in Singapore?
CPF_DATA.a:
You may appoint a person who is not based in Singapore as long as they meet the eligibility criteria.
  
 Your Trusted Contact must: 
 Be a CPF member aged 21 and above; 
 Be a Singapore Citizen or Singapore Permanent Resident (with Singpass account); and 
 Have both a Singapore-registered mobile number and email address registered with CPF Board

CPF_DATA.q: Can I submit more than one Trusted Contact appointment request within the same day?
CPF_DATA.a:
Yes, you can submit up to two Trusted Contact appointment requests within the same day, which is the maximum number of Trusted Contacts you can appoint.
  
 If you wish to submit a new Trusted Contact appointment request when you already have reached the maximum of two Trusted Contacts, you must first withdraw one of your pending requests or remove an existing Trusted Contact, before submitting a new appointment request.

CPF_DATA.q: Why do I need my appointee to accept the Trusted Contact appointment request?
CPF_DATA.a: A Trusted Contact will receive copies of your notifications when you make important transactions and updates in your CPF account. As the role of Trusted Contact is a voluntary one, your appointee must understand their role before accepting your appointment request.

CPF_DATA.q: I have submitted my Trusted Contact appointment request. How will my appointee be informed to accept my appointment request?
CPF_DATA.a: Notifications will be sent to your appointee via email, SMS and push notifications via the CPF Mobile app (if enabled) to inform them of your appointment request. While CPF Board will notify your appointee, we strongly encourage you to personally inform them of your appointment request beforehand. This helps to ensure that they are aware of the appointment request and understand their role as your Trusted Contact.

CPF_DATA.q: How long does my appointee have to accept my Trusted Contact appointment request?
CPF_DATA.a:
Your appointee has seven days to respond to your Trusted Contact appointment request, after which the request will automatically expire. If the request has expired, you will need to submit a new appointment request. 
  
 We strongly encourage you to personally inform the appointee about the appointment request to ensure that they are aware of the appointment request and understand their role as your Trusted Contact.

CPF_DATA.q: How can I check the status of my Trusted Contact appointment?
CPF_DATA.a:
You can check the status of your Trusted Contact appointment via your Activities page using your Singpass. 
  
 You may also view your appointed Trusted Contacts at the “Trusted Contacts” section under your Account settings. Only Trusted Contacts who have accepted your appointment request will be listed under “Your Trusted Contacts”.

CPF_DATA.q: Where can I see an overview of my Trusted Contact appointment and appointment requests?
CPF_DATA.a:
You may view your appointed Trusted Contacts at the “Trusted Contacts” section under your Account settings. Only Trusted Contacts who have accepted your appointment request will be listed under “Your Trusted Contacts”. For pending appointment requests, you may track the status via your Activities page. Pending requests have the “In-Progress” transaction status.
 
 If someone has appointed you as their Trusted Contacts, you will be able to see the pending request(s) under “Added you as a Trusted Contact” in the Account settings – Trusted Contacts section.

CPF_DATA.q: What should I do if I wish to amend my Trusted Contact appointment request?
CPF_DATA.a:
If your Trusted Contact appointee has not accepted your request and you wish to change the appointee, you can do so by withdrawing your appointment request and submitting a new one. 
  
 To withdraw the request:
 Go to your Activities page and locate the pending request that you wish to withdraw. The “Add or remove Trusted Contact” transaction should still be “In-Progress”.
 Click into the “In-progress” transaction record and click the “Withdraw this request” button at the bottom of the page.
 If your Trusted Contact request has been accepted, you will not be able to withdraw your appointment. Instead, you will need to remove the existing Trusted Contact appointment first. You may do so via the Add or remove Trusted Contact e-form before submitting a new appointment request.

CPF_DATA.q: I received a notification informing me that my Trusted Contact appointment was declined. What should I do?
CPF_DATA.a:
The notification was sent to inform you that the person you had tried to appoint as your Trusted Contact has declined your appointment request. You should try to contact your appointee to explain your request and help them understand their role as your Trusted Contact before submitting a new appointment request.
 
 Alternatively, you may consider choosing a different person to appoint as your Trusted Contact. We strongly encourage you to personally inform them of your appointment request beforehand. This helps to ensure that they are aware of the appointment request and understand their role as your Trusted Contact.

CPF_DATA.q: I received a notification stating my Trusted Contact appointment request has expired. What should I do?
CPF_DATA.a:
The notification was sent to inform you that the person you had tried to appoint as your Trusted Contact did not respond to your appointment request within seven days of your submission. 
 
 Your appointee must agree to be your Trusted Contact by accepting your appointment request at the “Trusted Contact” section under their Account settings. 
 
 If you wish to appoint the same person as your Trusted Contact, you should personally inform them before resubmitting your appointment request, so that they can respond promptly.
 
 Alternatively, you may consider choosing a different person to appoint as your Trusted Contact.

CPF_DATA.q: I encountered an error informing me that the name I keyed for my appointee was different from his NRIC/CPF Account Number. How can I resolve this?
CPF_DATA.a:
You should key in the full name of your appointee as per his NRIC. This includes any punctuation such as the comma (,), hyphen (-), and slash (/).
 
 However, you should exclude the Hanyu Pinyin, Alias and Married name. See examples of NRIC names.
 
 If you are still encountering problems in submitting your Trusted Contact appointment request, we recommend that you check the following:
 Check for the correct spacing of characters in the names of your appointee.
 For example: 
 John⎵Tan should not be written as John⎵⎵Tan or John⎵Tan⎵ 
 D/O should not be written as D / O, and likewise for S/O 
 Avoid copy-pasting the details from another program or app as there might be hidden characters that might get copied. Key in the appointee’s particulars manually instead. 
 If you are using an iOS device (such as iPhone or iPad), disable the “Smart Punctuation” feature in iOS (iOS device Settings > General > Keyboard > Turn off Smart Punctuation). This feature might create unrecognisable characters in the application form. 
 Avoid opening multiple browser tabs. Use only one browser tab and clear your browser cache. 
 Consider using a different device to submit your Trusted Contact appointment request if submission via your original device is still unsuccessful. 
 If you continue to encounter the same error despite the above, you can write to us via Singpass with the following details for assistance:
 A screenshot of the name of your appointee, 
 The time and date when the error occurred, and 
 Details of the device that you are using to submit your Trusted Contact appointment request.

CPF_DATA.q: I encountered an error informing me that the person I intend to appoint as my Trusted Contact has reached their appointment limit. What should I do?
CPF_DATA.a: The person you are trying to appoint is already a Trusted Contact of several other individuals. We recommend appointing someone else for better monitoring of your important CPF transactions or updates. If you still prefer to appoint this person, you can ask them to remove one of their existing appointments. However, do consider that spreading their attention across multiple members might affect their focus and effectiveness in spotting suspicious activities promptly.

CPF_DATA.q: I encountered an error informing me that the person I intend to appoint as my Trusted Contact cannot be appointed because their contact details are incomplete. What should I do?
CPF_DATA.a:
To better safeguard you from potential scams, your Trusted Contact must have both a Singapore-registered mobile number and an email address registered with CPF Board, as they will receive a copy of your transaction notifications whenever important CPF transactions or updates are made in your CPF account. 
 
 You should inform the person you are trying to appoint to update their contact details with CPF Board. If this is not possible, you should consider appointing a different person as your Trusted Contact instead.

CPF_DATA.q: I encountered an error informing me that the person I intend to appoint as my Trusted Contact is not eligible. What should I do?
CPF_DATA.a:
The person you are trying to appoint as your Trusted Contact is not eligible to use the service.
 
 Your Trusted Contact must:
 Be a CPF member aged 21 and above 
 Be a Singapore Citizen or Singapore Permanent Resident 
 Have both a Singapore-registered mobile number and email address registered with CPF Board
 Please consider appointing a different person as your Trusted Contact instead.

CPF_DATA.q: Why do I need to provide the Name and NRIC number of my appointee in the Trusted Contact appointment form?
CPF_DATA.a: Providing the Name and NRIC number of your appointee helps CPF Board correctly identify the person you wish to appoint as your Trusted Contact. CPF Board will also check if your appointee is eligible to be a Trusted Contact.

CPF_DATA.q: How can I make a Trusted Contact appointment if I failed the enhanced authentication verification?
CPF_DATA.a:
If you failed the enhanced authentication when trying to submit a Trusted Contact appointment request, please try again later.
 
 Otherwise, you may visit any CPF Service Centre with your intended appointee for assistance. Please schedule a visit to any CPF Service Centre as counter services are available by booking only.

CPF_DATA.q: My appointed Trusted Contact is not receiving a copy of my notification when I perform CPF transactions. Why is this so?
CPF_DATA.a:
The Trusted Contact service only sends notifications to your Trusted Contact for transactions below: 
 
 CPF lump sum withdrawals for immediate retirement needs 
 Contact details update 
 Bank account update 
 Daily withdrawal limit update 
 If your Trusted Contact did not receive a notification, it may be because the transaction you performed is not covered by the Trusted Contact service.
  
 However, if your Trusted Contact is not receiving notifications for any of the above transactions, please write to us via Singpass with the following details: 
  
 Name of your Trusted Contact 
 The type of transaction you performed, including the time and date 
 Screenshot of the notifications sent to you

CPF_DATA.q: How do I remove my appointed Trusted Contact?
CPF_DATA.a:
You can easily remove a Trusted Contact via the Add or remove Trusted Contact e-form. You will need to login with your Singpass when you begin the e-form.
 
 Before you proceed:
 Verify that your contact details registered with CPF Board are correct. If you need to update your contact details, you can do so via Account settings before returning to this form. 
 
 Steps to remove:
 To remove a Trusted Contact, select the ‘Remove a Trusted Contact’ option at Step 1 of the form.
 Next, select the Trusted Contact you wish to remove.
 Review your input before submitting the form. 
 What happens next:
 The removal of your Trusted Contact will take immediate effect.
 Notifications will be sent to both you and the Trusted Contact you have removed. 
 Important notes:
 You may need to verify your identity using enhanced authentication measures to complete the submission.

CPF_DATA.q: I encountered an error informing me that I cannot remove my appointed Trusted Contact because their contact details are incomplete. What should I do?
CPF_DATA.a:
Please inform the person you are trying to remove as your Trusted Contact to register both a Singapore-registered mobile number and an email address with CPF Board. Once your Trusted Contact has successfully updated their contact details, you may proceed to remove the Trusted Contact via the e-form.
 
 If this is not possible, you may write to us via Singpass with the following details:
  A screenshot of the error page, 
 The name and NRIC number of the Trusted Contact you wish to remove 
 You may also visit any CPF Service Centre to remove the Trusted Contact in person. Please schedule a visit to any CPF Service Centre, as counter services are available by booking only.

CPF_DATA.q: How long does it take to remove an appointed Trusted Contact?
CPF_DATA.a: The removal of your Trusted Contact will take immediate effect upon the successful submission of the Add or remove Trusted Contact e-form.

CPF_DATA.q: How can I check the status of my application to remove an appointed Trusted Contact?
CPF_DATA.a:
You can check the status of your Trusted Contact removal application via your Activities page using your Singpass. 
 
 The removal of your Trusted Contact will take immediate effect upon successful submission of the Add or remove Trusted Contact e-form. You may also refer to the “Trusted Contacts” section under your Account settings and check that the Trusted Contact you have removed is not shown under “Your Trusted Contacts”.

CPF_DATA.q: How can I remove a Trusted Contact appointment if I failed the enhanced authentication?
CPF_DATA.a:
If you fail the enhanced authentication when trying to remove a Trusted Contact, please try again later.
  
 Otherwise, you may visit any CPF Service Centre for assistance. Please schedule a visit to any CPF Service Centre as counter services are available by booking only.

CPF_DATA.q: I did not perform any removal for my Trusted Contact appointment. Why was my Trusted Contact appointment / appointment as a Trusted Contact removed by CPF Board?
CPF_DATA.a: The CPF Board may remove your appointed Trusted Contact or your appointment as a Trusted Contact when either you or your Trusted Contact are no longer eligible to use the service. You are advised to check with your appointer or your Trusted Contact if there has been a change in their circumstances affecting their eligibility.

CPF_DATA.q: What happens to my Trusted Contact appointment(s) upon my demise?
CPF_DATA.a: CPF Board will revoke your appointed Trusted Contact(s) as well as your appointment(s) as a Trusted Contact upon your demise.

CPF_DATA.q: I am a deputy/donee for a CPF member. How can I remove their Trusted Contact appointment(s)?
CPF_DATA.a:
As a deputy/donee, you can write to us via Singpass to request the removal of Trusted Contact on a member’s behalf, by providing the following documents: 
 
 For Donee appointed under a Lasting Power of Attorney (LPA): Submit the LPA document and a medical report certifying your donor’s loss of mental capacity
 For Deputy appointed by the Singapore Court: Submit the Deputyship court order

CPF_DATA.q: What happens if my appointed Trusted Contact loses their mental capacity?
CPF_DATA.a:
You should revoke their Trusted Contact appointment via the Add or remove Trusted Contact e-form when your appointed Trusted Contact loses their mental capacity. 
 
 You may also wish to submit a new appointment request to appoint a different person to be your Trusted Contact.

CPF_DATA.q: What is my role as a Trusted Contact?
CPF_DATA.a:
As an appointed Trusted Contact, you play a role in helping to protect your appointer from potential scams. You will receive a copy of the notifications sent to your appointer when important transactions and updates occur in their CPF account. By monitoring these notifications, you can help to spot and alert them to any suspicious activities in their CPF account that may not be performed by them. 
 
 Notifications from the following CPF services will be copied to you when triggered by transactions in your appointer’s CPF account: 
 CPF lump sum withdrawals for immediate retirement needs 
 Contact details update 
 Bank account update 
 Daily withdrawal limit update 
 Your appointer will still receive notifications at their own registered contact.
  
 It is important to note that as a Trusted Contact, you cannot transact or make changes to your appointer’s CPF account on their behalf as your appointer has sole control of their CPF account. Your role is limited to receiving a copy of their transaction notifications and alerting your appointer when you notice any unusual or unexpected transactions or updates made in their CPF account. Learn how to differentiate notifications you receive as a Trusted Contact from notifications meant for your own CPF account.

CPF_DATA.q: I am a Trusted Contact. Why am I not allowed to transact on my appointer’s behalf or view the CPF account balances and statements?
CPF_DATA.a:
As a Trusted Contact, you serve as an extra pair of eyes to help safeguard your appointer from potential scams. To meet this objective, you will receive a copy of the notifications whenever important transactions occur in your appointer’s CPF account.
 
 We strike a balance between enabling you to support your appointer and protecting their privacy. You can only receive key transaction information through notifications to help you monitor for suspicious transactions - you cannot view your appointer's full CPF account details, make transactions, or change any account settings on their behalf. This ensures you can help identify suspicious transactions while your appointer's account privacy remains protected.

CPF_DATA.q: How can I differentiate the notifications I receive as a Trusted Contact from notifications for my own CPF account?
CPF_DATA.a:
You can look out for the label in the notifications sent to you: 
 
 SMS Notifications: Begins with “(To Trusted Contact)” 
 Email: Contains “You are receiving this notification as an appointed Trusted Contact” 
 Push Notifications via CPF Mobile app (if enabled): Contains “You are receiving this notification as an appointed Trusted Contact of <Name of your appointer>”

CPF_DATA.q: How do I become a Trusted Contact for a loved one?
CPF_DATA.a:
Your loved one can appoint you as their Trusted Contact via the Add or remove Trusted Contact
 e-form. They will need to login with their Singpass when they begin the e-form. 
  
 To be a Trusted Contact, you must:
 Be a CPF member aged 21 or above; and
 Be a Singapore Citizen or Singapore Permanent Resident (with Singpass account); and
 Have both a Singapore-registered mobile number and email address registered with CPF Board. 
 Before accepting the role of a Trusted Contact, please review the Terms and Conditions of the Trusted Contact service and understand the role of a Trusted Contact. Please refer to the Trusted Contact information page for more details.

CPF_DATA.q: Why do I need to have both a Singapore-registered mobile number and email address registered with CPF Board to be a Trusted Contact?
CPF_DATA.a:
As an appointed Trusted Contact, you play an important role in helping to protect your appointer from potential scams. You will receive a copy of the notifications sent to your appointer when they make important transactions and updates to their CPF account.
 
 It is crucial for Trusted Contacts to spot and flag any unusual or unexpected activities in your appointer’s CPF account. As such, you would need to maintain both a Singapore-registered mobile number and email address with CPF Board, so that you can receive your appointers’ notifications via both channels and take timely action.

CPF_DATA.q: I received a notification informing me of a Trusted Contact appointment request. What should I do?
CPF_DATA.a:
Someone has requested you to be their Trusted Contact. Please verify that this appointment was correctly made by checking the requestor’s name.
 
 You can accept or decline the appointment request at the “Trusted Contacts” section under your Account settings.

CPF_DATA.q: A loved one has appointed me as their Trusted Contact. How do I accept?
CPF_DATA.a: You can accept the appointment request under “Added you as a Trusted Contact” in the “Trusted Contacts” section under your Account settings.

CPF_DATA.q: An unknown party has appointed me as their Trusted Contact. What should I do?
CPF_DATA.a: If you have received a Trusted Contact appointment request from a person not known to you, you can decline the request at the “Trusted Contacts” section under your Account settings.

CPF_DATA.q: Can I be a Trusted Contact for someone who is not my next-of-kin?
CPF_DATA.a: Yes. You can be the Trusted Contact for someone who is not your next-of-kin if they appoint you. You must also meet these eligibility criteria to be a Trusted Contact.

CPF_DATA.q: I received a notification informing me that a pending Trusted Contact appointment request has been withdrawn. What should I do?
CPF_DATA.a: No further action is required from you as the pending appointment request has been withdrawn by the appointer.

CPF_DATA.q: Will the appointer be informed when I accept or decline their Trusted Contact appointment request?
CPF_DATA.a: Yes, the person who has appointed you as their Trusted Contact will be notified via email, SMS and push notifications (if enabled) when you accept or decline their Trusted Contact appointment request.

CPF_DATA.q: I am the appointed Trusted Contact of someone who has lost their mental capacity. Will my appointment as the Trusted Contact be affected?
CPF_DATA.a:
If a person who appointed you loses their mental capacity, your appointment as their Trusted Contact will not be affected and remains valid.
 
 A Court-appointed Deputy or Donee appointed under a Lasting Power of Attorney (LPA) are legally empowered to manage the affairs of your appointer (who has lost his mental capacity). They may remove you as the Trusted Contact if they deem it necessary and you will be informed accordingly. If you wish to remove yourself from the Trusted Contact’s list, you may do so via the Remove yourself from your loved ones’ Trusted Contact list e-form.

CPF_DATA.q: I no longer wish to be appointed as a Trusted Contact. How do I remove my appointment?
CPF_DATA.a:
You can easily remove your appointment as a Trusted Contact via the Remove yourself from your loved ones’ Trusted Contact list e-form. You will need to login with your Singpass when you begin the e-form.
  
 Before you proceed:
 Verify that your contact details registered with CPF Board are correct. If you need to update your contact details, you can do so via Account settings before returning to this form. 
 
 How to remove yourself as Trusted Contact:
 Select the person from whose Trusted Contact list you wish to remove yourself. 
 Review your selection before submitting the form. 
 What happens next:
 The removal will take immediate effect after successful submission. 
 Both you and your appointer (from whom you have removed your appointment as Trusted Contact) will be notified. 
 Important notes: 
 You may need to verify your identity using enhanced authentication measures to complete the submission.

CPF_DATA.q: I encountered an error informing me that I cannot remove my appointment as Trusted Contact because my appointer’s contact details are incomplete. Can CPF Board assist to remove my appointment?
CPF_DATA.a:
Please inform your appointer to update their contact details with CPF Board. Once your appointer has successfully updated their contact details, you may proceed to remove your appointment as a Trusted Contact via the Remove yourself from your loved ones’ Trusted Contact list e-form.
 
 If this is not possible, you may write to us via Singpass with the following details:
  A screenshot of the error page, 
 The name and NRIC number of the appointer you wish to remove 
 You may also visit any CPF Service Centre to remove your Trusted Contact appointment in person. Please schedule a visit to any CPF Service Centre as counter services are available by booking only.

CPF_DATA.q: How long does it take to remove my appointment as a Trusted Contact?
CPF_DATA.a: You will be removed as a Trusted Contact with immediate effect upon a successful submission.

CPF_DATA.q: How can I check the status of my application to remove my appointment as a Trusted Contact?
CPF_DATA.a:
You can check the status of your application to remove your appointment as a Trusted Contact via your Activities page using your Singpass.
 
 Upon successful submission of the Remove yourself from your loved ones’ Trusted Contact list e-form, your name will be removed from the “Trusted Contacts” section under your Account settings.

CPF_DATA.q: How can I remove my appointment as a Trusted Contact if I failed the enhanced authentication?
CPF_DATA.a:
If you failed the enhanced authentication when trying to remove yourself as a Trusted Contact, please try again later.
 
 Otherwise, you may visit any CPF Service Centre for assistance. Please schedule a visit to any CPF Service Centre as counter services are available by booking only.

CPF_DATA.q: As a Trusted Contact, what should I do upon receiving notifications regarding transactions on my appointer’s CPF account?
CPF_DATA.a:
As a Trusted Contact, you will receive copies of your appointer’s notifications whenever important transactions occur in your appointer’s CPF account. When you receive these notifications, check with your appointer to confirm they made the transaction.
 
 If unsure whether your appointer may have fallen for a scam, STOP and CHECK with 24/7 ScamShield anti-scam helpline via 1799. To learn more about common scam signs and scam types, please visit the ScamShield website.
 
 If your appointer has fallen victim to a scam, advise or guide them to take the following steps immediately:
 Alert the bank to freeze any affected bank account(s) 
 Call Singpass’ 24/7 helpdesk at +65 6335 3533 to reset their Singpass 
 Activate the CPF Withdrawal Lock to disable online CPF withdrawals (applicable for members aged 55 and above) 
 Lodge a police report and inform CPF Board

CPF_DATA.q: Why is the Trusted Contact notified when the appointer makes a withdrawal transaction?
CPF_DATA.a:
Scammers may deceive victims into withdrawing their CPF savings or attempt to withdraw victims’ CPF savings if they obtain unauthorised access to the victims’ CPF accounts. Therefore, it is crucial for Trusted Contacts to be alerted when such a transaction is made.
 
 Learn more about scammers’ latest tactics, and tips to help your loved ones safeguard themselves against scams.

CPF_DATA.q: Why is the Trusted Contact notified when the appointer updates their bank account, contact details or daily withdrawal limit?
CPF_DATA.a:
Scammers may deceive victims into updating their bank account, contact details or daily withdrawal limit. They may also attempt to update this information if they obtain unauthorised access to the victims’ CPF accounts. Therefore, it is crucial for Trusted Contacts to be alerted when such transactions are made.
 
 Learn more about scammers’ latest tactics and tips to help your loved ones safeguard themselves against scams.

CPF_DATA.q: Can CPF Board notify Trusted Contacts about other types of transactions made by their appointers?
CPF_DATA.a: Only notifications for critical transactions or updates that may expose an account to higher scam risk (e.g. when a withdrawal is made, or when bank account information is changed) are copied to Trusted Contacts. This avoids overwhelming Trusted Contacts with less critical notifications regarding their appointer’s account.
